title: Document Template Creator
prompt_text: Act as a document design specialist for enterprise communications. Create a reusable template structure for a [DOCUMENT_TYPE: e.g., project charter, business case, status report, RFP response, meeting minutes]. The template should include: (1) a professional header section with placeholders for title, date, author, version, and classification, (2) a table of contents with standard sections for this document type, (3) clearly labeled placeholder text in each section explaining what content goes there and approximate length, (4) a standard footer with page numbers and confidentiality notice, (5) formatting guidance — heading styles, table formats, and callout boxes to use. Design this for a financial services audience where clarity, compliance, and professionalism are critical. Include a "How to Use This Template" section at the top.
description: Creates professional, reusable document templates with structured placeholders, formatting guidance, and compliance-ready layouts.
tips: Save completed templates to your organization's SharePoint template library for team-wide access. Use Copilot in Word to populate templates with content from source documents.

title: Employee Engagement Action Plan
prompt_text: Act as an employee relations specialist with expertise in workplace conflict resolution and compliance. Based on [SURVEY_RESULTS_OR_FEEDBACK], create an engagement action plan: 1) Key Findings Summary, 2) Root Cause Analysis for bottom 3 scores, 3) Action Items (table: Theme | Action | Owner | Timeline | Success Metric), 4) Communication Plan to employees, 5) Follow-up measurement approach. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Turns engagement survey data into concrete improvements with accountability and measurement.
tips: Address the top 3 issues — do not try to fix everything at once. Communicate back to employees what you heard and what you are doing about it.

title: Summarize Inbox
prompt_text: Act as an executive assistant specialist with expertise in inbox management and prioritization. I've received [NUMBER] emails about [TOPIC/PERIOD]: [EMAIL_SUMMARIES]. Summarize the inbox: what's the overall message, key concerns, and action items? Do I need to respond to each?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Distills email volume into clear takeaways and actions.
tips: Copilot works best with message subjects and key excerpts; full emails not needed. Flag urgent/escalated items.

title: Meeting Transcript Summarizer
prompt_text: Act as a meeting intelligence specialist who transforms raw transcripts into structured knowledge. Summarize this meeting transcript for [MEETING_TYPE: e.g., project standup, leadership review, client call, strategy session]. Structure as: (1) Meeting Metadata — date, duration, attendees, purpose, (2) Key Discussion Points — 3-5 main topics discussed, with the essential context for each (not just topic names but what was SAID about each), (3) Decisions Made — specific decisions with who made them and any conditions attached, (4) Action Items — table: Action | Owner | Deadline | Context/Notes, (5) Risks and Concerns Raised — issues flagged by participants with attribution, (6) Parking Lot — topics mentioned but deferred. Keep the summary under [WORD_COUNT: default 500] words. Think through what someone who missed this meeting absolutely needs to know. Verify that every named action has an owner and that no decisions are attributed incorrectly.
description: Transforms meeting transcripts into structured summaries with decisions, actions, and risk flagging.
tips: Provide the full transcript including speaker labels. Add "Focus on [SPECIFIC_TOPIC]" if the meeting covered many topics. For client-facing meetings, add "Flag any commitments made to the client."

title: Meeting Minutes and Decision Log
prompt_text: Act as a corporate secretary with expertise in formal meeting documentation. Create official meeting minutes from this [TRANSCRIPT/NOTES] for [MEETING_NAME]. Follow formal minutes structure: (1) Header — meeting name, date, time, location/platform, (2) Attendance — present, absent, guests (with affiliations), (3) Agenda Review — confirm agenda was followed; note any additions, (4) Minutes of Previous Meeting — approval status and any corrections, (5) Business Items — for each agenda item: discussion summary (key points only, not verbatim), motion/proposal if any, vote/decision outcome, dissenting views if recorded, (6) Action Items — table: # | Action | Responsible | Due Date | Status, (7) Next Meeting — date, time, proposed agenda items, (8) Adjournment — time meeting ended. Use neutral, objective language. Attribute positions to roles rather than individuals where appropriate for sensitivity. Verify that every agenda item has a documented outcome (decision, deferral, or action assigned).
description: Creates formal meeting minutes with governance-ready structure, decision documentation, and action tracking.
tips: Provide the meeting agenda for structure alignment. Add "This is a [BOARD/COMMITTEE/WORKING GROUP] meeting" for appropriate formality level. For regulated environments, add "Minutes must comply with [GOVERNANCE_STANDARD]."

title: Cross-Meeting Action Tracker
prompt_text: Act as a project coordination specialist with expertise in meeting follow-up and accountability. I have action items from [NUMBER] recent meetings. Build a consolidated action tracker: (1) Master Action List — table: Action # | Description | Owner | Source Meeting | Date Assigned | Due Date | Status (Not Started/In Progress/Complete/Overdue/Blocked), (2) Overdue Items — actions past their due date: who owns them, how overdue, and suggested follow-up message, (3) Upcoming Deadlines — actions due in the next [PERIOD: 1 week / 2 weeks], (4) Owner Summary — group by person: what each person owes, ordered by due date, (5) Carried Forward Items — actions that have been reassigned or deferred multiple times (pattern of avoidance), (6) Completion Rate — percentage of actions completed on time by owner and by meeting type. Draft a brief follow-up email for each person with overdue items: professional, specific, and constructive. Verify that no action items have been lost between meetings and that completed items are properly closed out.
description: Consolidates meeting actions across sessions with overdue tracking, owner summaries, and follow-up drafting.
tips: Provide action items with their source meeting dates. Add "Our meeting cadence is [WEEKLY/BIWEEKLY/MONTHLY]" for timeline context. For persistent overdue items, add "Escalation path is [MANAGER/SPONSOR]."

title: Create from Notes
prompt_text: Act as a presentation design strategist with expertise in executive storytelling. I have meeting notes/talking points: [NOTES]. Create a [LENGTH]-slide presentation outline for [AUDIENCE]. Structure: [STRUCTURE: narrative arc, problem-solution, etc.]. Key messages: [MESSAGES]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Scaffolds presentation from raw notes quickly.
tips: Provide main talking points; Copilot will suggest slide structure. Use this as outline; you fill in details and visuals.

title: Standardized Report Generator from Data
prompt_text: Act as a reporting specialist with expertise in standardized business reporting. Generate a [REPORT_TYPE: e.g., monthly operations report, quarterly compliance report, annual review, incident report] from this data: [DATA_SOURCE]. Follow our standard report structure: (1) Executive Summary — 3-5 sentences covering the key takeaway, (2) Scorecard/Dashboard — table: Metric | Target | Actual | Status (RAG) | Trend, (3) Narrative Sections — for each reporting area: performance summary, notable events, risks, and actions taken, (4) Trend Analysis — compare to previous [PERIOD] with commentary on significant changes, (5) Appendix — supporting data tables and methodology notes. Formatting requirements: [SPECIFY: e.g., use corporate template, include page numbers, branded header/footer]. Verify that all metrics are calculated consistently with prior reports and that narrative statements are supported by the data shown. Flag any data points that appear anomalous compared to historical patterns.
description: Generates standardized reports from data following organizational templates with consistency checks.
tips: Provide a previous report as a formatting reference. Add "Our reporting cadence is [FREQUENCY] and this covers [PERIOD]" for context. For compliance reports, specify the regulatory framework driving the reporting requirements.

title: KPI Dashboard Builder
prompt_text: Act as a senior analytics specialist with expertise in data-driven decision making. Build a KPI dashboard analyzing [DATASET]: 1) Define top 10 KPIs with calculation logic, 2) Current Period Performance vs. Target, 3) Trend Analysis (MoM and QoQ and YoY), 4) Drill-down by [DIMENSIONS] (region/product/team), 5) Alert Thresholds (red/yellow/green), 6) Executive summary of findings. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Creates an interactive KPI framework from raw data with automated status indicators.
tips: Define what green/yellow/red means for each KPI before building. Include leading indicators alongside lagging ones. Update targets quarterly.

title: Email-to-Proposal Conversion
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer and communications strategist. From this email exchange about [TOPIC], extract the proposal and expand it into a formal proposal document for [DECISION_MAKER]. Include: executive summary, detailed scope, timeline, investment, and next steps. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Escalates informal agreements into formal documents for contracts and approval.
tips: Ensure all key deal terms are in the email thread; Copilot will pull and structure them. Useful before legal review.

title: Post-Mortem / Retrospective Template
prompt_text: Act as a continuous improvement specialist with expertise in retrospective facilitation. Facilitate a post-mortem for [INCIDENT_OR_PROJECT]: 1) Timeline of Events, 2) What Went Well (keep doing), 3) What Went Wrong (stop doing), 4) Root Causes (not just symptoms), 5) Action Items (table: Action | Owner | Due Date | Priority), 6) Lessons Learned for future reference. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Turns failures and successes into organizational learning with clear accountability for improvements.
tips: No blame — focus on systems and processes not individuals. Capture actions with deadlines or they will not happen. Share findings broadly.

title: Project Health Dashboard
prompt_text: Act as a certified project manager with expertise in stakeholder communication. Build a project status notebook for [PROJECT_NAME]: 1) Executive Summary with RAG status, 2) Milestone Tracker (table: Milestone | Due Date | Status | Owner), 3) Risk Register (Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation), 4) Budget and Timeline Trends, 5) Blockers and Next Steps. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Live project tracking in Copilot Notebooks with real-time dashboard-style reporting fed by project data.
tips: Link to project management tools or paste snapshots. Copilot will structure and highlight risks. Update weekly for best results.

title: Project Status Report
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. Summarize the status of [PROJECT]: [DESCRIPTION]. Report on: progress (% complete, key milestones hit), blockers, risks, and next 2 weeks of work. Format as: Summary | Progress | Risks | Next Steps. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Standardizes status communication for governance and leadership visibility.
tips: Use a template each week for consistency; Copilot will learn your project context. Useful for steering committee reports.

title: Executive Summary Generator
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. Review the attached [DOCUMENT_TYPE] and create a 1-page executive summary highlighting: key findings, business implications, recommended actions, and any risks. Organize by priority and use bullet points for clarity. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Distills lengthy documents into actionable exec summaries for leadership review.
tips: Copilot works best with structured documents; for unstructured text, provide context clues about audience and use case. Avoid asking for "all details in summary format" as this defeats the purpose.

title: Meeting to Action Items
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. I have a meeting transcript from [MEETING_TYPE] about [TOPIC]. Extract: who owns each action, deadlines, blockers, and decisions made. Format as a table with Owner | Action | Deadline | Status. Flag any missing accountability. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Converts meeting notes into trackable action items with clear ownership and deadlines.
tips: Mention specific roles or names in the transcript if available; Copilot will pick them up more reliably. For recurring meetings, ask about standing items versus new actions.

title: Email Thread Summarizer
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. Summarize this email thread: identify the original problem, proposals offered, objections raised, and current status. Who still needs to respond? What is the decision point?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Distills long email chains into decision-ready briefs for leaders.
tips: Copy/paste the entire thread; Copilot will follow the conversation flow. Useful before jumping into an ongoing thread to get up to speed.

title: Step-by-Step Implementation Guide
prompt_text: Act as a senior implementation consultant with expertise in enterprise change delivery. I need a step-by-step implementation guide. Goal: [GOAL]. Current state: [CURRENT_STATE]. Desired end state: [DESIRED_END_STATE]. Available tools and systems: [TOOLS]. Build the guide as: (1) Prerequisites Checklist — what must be true before starting: access permissions, data readiness, approvals, dependencies resolved, (2) Implementation Strategy — recommended approach (phased vs. big-bang) with justification based on risk and complexity, (3) Detailed Steps — numbered sequence: Step # | Action (specific and concrete) | Owner/Role | Estimated Duration | Input Needed | Output/Deliverable | Verification (how to confirm this step is complete), (4) Decision Points — where the path branches based on findings or results, with criteria for each option, (5) Success Criteria — measurable indicators that the implementation is complete and working correctly, (6) Rollback Plan — how to reverse each major step if something goes wrong, with trigger conditions for rollback. Think through what could go wrong at each step. Verify that the guide could be followed by someone unfamiliar with the process — no assumed knowledge, no undefined acronyms, no "you know what to do" shortcuts.
description: Creates detailed implementation guides with prerequisites, phased steps, decision points, and rollback plans.
tips: Describe the current state and desired end state clearly. Add "The person following this guide has [EXPERIENCE_LEVEL] with [TOOLS]" for appropriate detail calibration. For complex implementations, add "We need a pilot phase before full rollout."

title: Options and Recommendations Engine
prompt_text: Act as a strategy advisor presenting options and recommendations. I need to decide on [DECISION_TOPIC]. Context: [CONTEXT]. Constraints: [CONSTRAINTS: budget, timeline, resources, risk appetite]. Present options: (1) Option Identification — identify 3-4 viable approaches (include the "do nothing" option as a baseline if relevant), (2) Options Matrix — table: Option | Description | Pros | Cons | Cost Estimate | Timeline | Risk Level | Confidence, (3) Evaluation Criteria — the factors that matter most for this decision, weighted by importance: Criterion | Weight | Option A Score | Option B Score | Option C Score, (4) Weighted Recommendation — which option scores highest, with clear reasoning, (5) Implementation Implications — what choosing each option means in practice: resources needed, dependencies, organizational impact, (6) Decision Framework — what additional information would change the recommendation, and what should trigger revisiting this decision. Think through second-order effects: what does each option enable or foreclose in the future? Verify that your evaluation criteria are independent (no double-counting) and that your recommendation is consistent with the weighted scores.
description: Presents structured options with weighted evaluation, recommendations, and implementation implications.
tips: Be specific about constraints and priorities. Add "Our decision-making style is [CONSENSUS/EXECUTIVE/COMMITTEE]" for appropriate framing. For time-sensitive decisions, add "We need to decide by [DATE] because [REASON]."

title: Process Automation Opportunity Identifier
prompt_text: Act as a process automation specialist with expertise in identifying high-value automation opportunities. Analyze this [PROCESS_DESCRIPTION or WORKFLOW] for automation potential: (1) Task Inventory — break down the process into individual tasks: Task | Current Actor (human/system) | Frequency | Duration | Complexity | Rule-Based? (Y/N), (2) Automation Candidates — tasks that are rule-based, repetitive, and high-volume: table: Task | Automation Type (RPA/API/AI/Workflow Rule) | Feasibility (Easy/Moderate/Hard) | Time Savings per Instance | Annual Volume, (3) ROI Estimate — for top 5 candidates: Current Cost (time × frequency × labor rate) vs. Automation Cost (implementation + maintenance), (4) Quick Wins — automations that can be implemented within [TIMEFRAME] with existing tools, (5) Dependencies and Prerequisites — what needs to be true for each automation to work (data quality, system access, process standardization), (6) Recommended Sequence — implementation order based on: quick-win value, dependency chain, and organizational readiness. Think through what should NOT be automated: judgment-heavy decisions, exceptions requiring empathy, processes still in flux. Verify that your time savings estimates are realistic and account for exception handling.
description: Identifies and prioritizes process automation opportunities with ROI estimation and implementation sequencing.
tips: Describe the process in detail, including volume and frequency. Add "Our available automation tools are [TOOLS: Power Automate, Copilot Studio, RPA platform]" for technology-appropriate recommendations. Start with your highest-volume, most-repetitive processes.

title: Next Steps and Milestone Planner
prompt_text: Act as a project planning specialist who reduces ambiguity and creates clear forward momentum. Based on [CURRENT_SITUATION: what just happened, what we just decided, where we are in the process], create a concrete next-steps plan: (1) Immediate Actions (Next 48 hours) — the 3-5 things that must happen RIGHT NOW: table: Action | Owner | Deadline | Why Urgent, (2) Short-Term Milestones (Next 2 weeks) — key deliverables and checkpoints: table: Milestone | Description | Owner | Date | Success Criteria | Dependencies, (3) Decisions Needed — questions that must be answered to keep moving: Who decides? | By when? | What information is needed? | What happens if no decision?, (4) Risks to the Plan — what could prevent these next steps from happening, with pre-emptive mitigation, (5) Communication Actions — who needs to be informed of what, and by when, (6) First Checkpoint — when should we reconvene to check progress, and what should be true by then. Think through: Is anything blocking us right now that we haven't addressed? Are the right people assigned to the right tasks? Verify that every action has a specific, named owner (not a team or department) and a concrete deadline (not "ASAP" or "when possible").
description: Creates concrete next-steps plans with immediate actions, milestones, decision points, and accountability.
tips: Describe where you are in the process and what just happened. Add "Our biggest concern right now is [CONCERN]" to focus the plan. For post-meeting planning, add "The meeting attendees who can take actions are [NAMES]."

title: Approval Tracking and Aging Dashboard
prompt_text: Act as a governance operations analyst with expertise in approval workflow optimization. Build an approval tracking dashboard from this data: (1) Pipeline Overview — total pending approvals by type, stage, and age bracket (0-2 days, 3-5 days, 6-10 days, 10+ days), (2) Approver Performance — table: Approver | Pending Items | Avg Response Time | Oldest Item | Approval Rate | Rejection Rate, (3) Bottleneck Analysis — which approvers or stages consistently exceed SLA, with pattern identification (day of week, request type, amount range), (4) Aging Report — all items exceeding [SLA_DAYS] days, sorted by business impact: table: Request ID | Type | Amount | Requester | Approver | Days Pending | Business Impact, (5) Reminder Queue — items that should receive an automated reminder today, with suggested message. Think through the difference between approvals that are slow because of complexity vs. those that are slow because of inaction. Verify that your SLA calculations exclude weekends and holidays.
description: Tracks approval pipeline health with approver performance metrics, bottleneck analysis, and aging-based escalation.
tips: Provide SLA targets per approval type. Add "Business-critical approvals include [TYPES]" to prioritize the aging report. For organizations with multiple approval platforms, consolidate data before analysis.

title: Blocked Item Escalation Framework
prompt_text: Act as a process governance specialist with expertise in escalation management. Review this set of blocked/stalled approval items and create an escalation action plan: (1) Blocked Item Inventory — table: Item | Type | Blocked Since | Blocker (person or condition) | Reason for Block | Business Impact if Unresolved, (2) Root Cause Classification — categorize each block: missing information, approver unavailable, policy ambiguity, system issue, or deliberate hold, (3) Resolution Actions — for each blocked item: specific action needed, who should take it, recommended deadline, and what happens if not resolved, (4) Escalation Recommendations — items that should be escalated to the next management level, with a brief justification for each, (5) Systemic Fixes — patterns that suggest process or policy changes could prevent future blocks. Draft an escalation email template for the top 3 most critical blocked items: concise, respectful, with clear ask and deadline. Verify that your resolution actions are specific enough to be actionable (not "follow up" but "provide the missing cost center code for PO #12345").
description: Creates escalation action plans for blocked approvals with root cause analysis and resolution tracking.
tips: Provide the SLA for each approval type and the current aging data. Add "Our escalation culture is [FORMAL/INFORMAL]" to calibrate tone. For politically sensitive escalations, add "Frame as process support, not blame."

title: Lease Abstraction
prompt_text: Act as a commercial real estate analyst with expertise in lease management and portfolio optimization. Summarize this lease [LEASE_NAME/REFERENCE]: landlord, tenant, sq ft, rent/year, term (start-end), renewal options, key clauses (options, escalations, maintenance). Flag any unusual terms. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Extracts key lease data for portfolio management and decision-making.
tips: Use lease text or summary; Copilot will synthesize. Useful for rent payment planning and renewal calendars.

title: Quarterly Portfolio Review
prompt_text: Act as a commercial real estate analyst with expertise in lease management and portfolio optimization. Prepare a quarterly real estate portfolio review: occupancy, cost, lease expiries, key issues, and recommendations. Audience: CFO/CEO. Include: YoY comparison, trend analysis, and action items. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Executive briefing on real estate performance and strategic initiatives.
tips: Use portfolio data, lease schedule, and market intelligence; Copilot will synthesize. Flag lease renewals and expiries coming due.

title: Lease Portfolio Review
prompt_text: Act as a facilities management expert with expertise in operations and sustainability. Consolidate lease portfolio: 1) Portfolio Summary (total sq ft and annual rent and expiration profile), 2) Lease Schedule (table: Location | Sq Ft | Rate | Expiration | Renewal Status), 3) Upcoming Expirations (next 24 months), 4) Renegotiation Opportunities, 5) Risk Summary. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Tracks full portfolio and flags renewal deadlines to avoid service disruptions or missed negotiation windows.
tips: Export from lease management system (Yardi or Argus or Excel). Copilot will organize and highlight risks. Track renewal status quarterly.

title: Utility Tracking
prompt_text: Act as a facilities management expert with expertise in operations and sustainability. [UTILITY_DATA in Excel: monthly electric, water, gas for each facility]. Summarize: total cost, cost per sq ft, trends, and seasonal patterns. Flag any anomalies (usage spikes, billing errors). Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Monitors utility costs for efficiency and anomaly detection.
tips: Benchmark vs. industry; Copilot will spot outliers. Use for efficiency planning.

title: Banner Design for Communications
prompt_text: Act as a digital communications designer. Create a professional banner for [CHANNEL: e.g., SharePoint intranet, email newsletter, Teams announcement, event page]. The banner should: (1) feature [TOPIC/THEME], (2) use a [WIDTH x HEIGHT or aspect ratio] format optimized for [CHANNEL], (3) incorporate our brand visual identity, (4) include minimal text — just [HEADLINE] and optionally [SUBHEADING]. The tone should be [TONE: e.g., inspiring, informative, celebratory]. Ensure the design works at both full size and when scaled down for mobile viewing.
description: Designs banners optimized for various digital channels — intranet, email, Teams — using Copilot Create.
tips: Specify exact dimensions when possible. SharePoint banners work best at 16:5 ratio. Email banners at 600px wide. Always preview on mobile.

title: Brand Kit Compliance Checker
prompt_text: Act as a brand compliance auditor for a large financial institution. Review this [DOCUMENT_TYPE: e.g., presentation, newsletter, one-pager, social post] against our brand guidelines. Check for: (1) correct logo usage and placement, (2) approved color palette adherence (flag any off-brand colors with hex codes), (3) typography consistency — correct fonts for headlines, body, and captions, (4) image quality and appropriateness for our regulated industry, (5) tone of voice alignment with brand guidelines. Produce a compliance scorecard: list each element checked, its status (compliant/non-compliant/needs review), and specific remediation steps for any issues found. Prioritize fixes by visibility impact.
description: Audits documents and designs against organizational brand guidelines using Copilot's Brand Kit and Brand Checker features.
tips: For best results, ensure your Brand Kit is configured in the M365 admin center with logos, colors, fonts, and voice guidelines. PowerPoint has built-in Brand Checker that automates some of these checks.

title: AI Image Generation for Business
prompt_text: Act as a visual communications specialist with expertise in corporate imagery. Generate a professional image for [PURPOSE: e.g., internal presentation, newsletter header, team announcement]. The image should: (1) convey [CONCEPT/THEME], (2) use a [STYLE: e.g., modern minimalist, corporate professional, warm and approachable] aesthetic, (3) be suitable for [PLACEMENT: e.g., PowerPoint slide, email banner, Teams background]. Avoid any text in the image. Ensure the visual is appropriate for a professional financial services environment — no controversial imagery, no identifiable faces unless specified. Describe what you see in the generated image so I can assess fit before using it.
description: Generates professional AI images via Copilot Create for business communications, presentations, and internal content.
tips: Be specific about style and context. Copilot generates 4 variations — pick the best fit. Regenerate with refined prompts if needed. Enterprise Brand Kits auto-apply when configured.

title: Brand-Compliant Visual Asset
prompt_text: Act as a brand compliance officer and visual designer. Using our organization's Brand Kit, generate a [ASSET_TYPE: e.g., social media graphic, event banner, newsletter header] for [EVENT/PURPOSE]. Ensure the output adheres to our brand guidelines: approved color palette, typography, and logo placement. The design should communicate [KEY_MESSAGE] to [TARGET_AUDIENCE]. After generating, review the asset against brand standards and flag any elements that may need manual adjustment for full compliance.
description: Creates visual assets that automatically leverage the organization's Brand Kit for consistent branding across all outputs.
tips: Brand Kits must be configured by your Brand Manager in the M365 admin center. If your output looks off-brand, check that the kit is published and active.

title: Image Editing and Refinement
prompt_text: Act as a photo editor and visual refinement specialist. I have an AI-generated image that needs adjustments. Please help me refine it by: (1) [EDIT_TYPE: e.g., remove the background, change the background to a corporate office setting, crop to a 16:9 aspect ratio, adjust the color tone to be warmer/cooler, add a professional filter]. (2) After making the edit, describe what changed so I can confirm it matches my intent. (3) If the edit doesn't achieve what I want, suggest 2-3 alternative approaches I could try. Note: Copilot can edit AI-generated images but cannot edit uploaded personal photos — if I need that, suggest the best alternative workflow.
description: Refines and edits AI-generated images using Copilot Create's built-in editing tools — crop, background removal, filters, and style adjustments.
tips: Image editing in Copilot works on AI-generated images. For editing personal photos, use Microsoft Designer or PowerPoint's built-in image tools instead.

title: Infographic Builder
prompt_text: Act as an information designer with expertise in data visualization for executive audiences. Create an infographic that visualizes [TOPIC/DATA]. Structure it as: (1) a clear headline summarizing the key insight, (2) [NUMBER] data points or sections arranged in a logical flow (top-to-bottom or left-to-right), (3) icons or visual metaphors for each section to aid quick comprehension, (4) a call-to-action or key takeaway at the bottom. Use our brand colors. The infographic should tell a story — a busy executive should grasp the core message in under 10 seconds. Explain your design rationale so I can refine.
description: Creates data-driven infographics for executive presentations, internal communications, and reports using Copilot Create.
tips: Keep data points to 5-7 for maximum impact. Provide actual numbers/stats in your prompt for best results. You can paste data directly or reference attached files.

title: Newsletter Design and Content
prompt_text: Act as an internal communications specialist and newsletter designer. Create a [FREQUENCY: weekly/monthly/quarterly] newsletter for [AUDIENCE: e.g., department team, all-staff, leadership group] covering [TOPIC/THEME]. Structure it as: (1) a catchy headline and brief editorial intro (2-3 sentences), (2) [NUMBER] content sections — each with a headline, 50-75 word summary, and a call-to-action or link placeholder, (3) a "Quick Hits" sidebar with 3-4 bullet-point updates, (4) a closing note with contact info or feedback prompt. Suggest a visual layout that works in both email and SharePoint formats. Keep the tone [TONE: e.g., informative and warm, professional and concise].
description: Creates structured newsletter content and layout guidance for internal communications via email or SharePoint.
tips: Use Copilot in Word to draft the content, then Copilot Create or Designer to produce the visual layout. For recurring newsletters, save your first issue as a template.

title: Presentation Design Enhancement
prompt_text: Act as a presentation design consultant who specializes in executive-ready slide decks. Review my existing presentation about [TOPIC] and enhance it by: (1) identifying slides with too much text and suggesting visual alternatives (diagrams, charts, icons, timelines), (2) recommending a consistent visual hierarchy — headline font, body font, accent colors — aligned with our brand, (3) restructuring the narrative flow using a [FRAMEWORK: e.g., situation-complication-resolution, problem-solution-benefit, past-present-future] structure, (4) adding speaker notes that capture key talking points for each slide. Flag any slides that could be consolidated or removed entirely. The deck should be presentation-ready for a [AUDIENCE: e.g., board meeting, team standup, client pitch].
description: Transforms rough or text-heavy presentations into polished, visually compelling slide decks using Copilot in PowerPoint.
tips: Use Copilot in PowerPoint's Agent Mode for iterative design refinement. It can restructure content, convert bullets to visuals, and apply consistent styling across slides.

title: Poster Design Generator
prompt_text: Act as a graphic designer specializing in corporate event materials. Design a professional poster for [EVENT/INITIATIVE: e.g., town hall, wellness week, charity drive, team offsite]. Include: (1) a compelling headline that captures attention, (2) key details — date, time, location, RSVP info, (3) visual elements that match a [TONE: e.g., energetic, formal, celebratory] mood, (4) our brand colors and styling. The poster should be suitable for both digital distribution (email, Teams) and print (letter/A4 size). Generate 2-3 design concepts so I can choose the best direction.
description: Creates professional event posters using Copilot Create's poster design capabilities with brand-consistent styling.
tips: Use the Create hub (create.microsoft.com) for the best poster design experience. You can iterate on designs with follow-up prompts like "make the headline bigger" or "use a warmer color scheme."

title: Video Storyboard from Presentation
prompt_text: Act as a video production consultant. I have a PowerPoint presentation about [TOPIC] that I need to convert into a short video (2-3 minutes). Help me: (1) identify the key slides that should become video scenes, (2) write a narration script for each scene (conversational but professional tone), (3) suggest transitions between scenes, (4) recommend where to add emphasis — pauses, text overlays, or b-roll suggestions, (5) define the opening hook and closing call-to-action. The video will be created using Copilot Create's video feature (Clipchamp integration). Ensure the script pacing matches a [SPEED: e.g., measured executive pace, energetic marketing pace] delivery.
description: Plans and scripts short videos from existing presentations using Copilot Create's Clipchamp-powered video feature.
tips: Copilot Create video currently requires a PowerPoint file as input. Prepare your deck first, then use this prompt to plan the video conversion. Keep videos under 3 minutes for maximum engagement.

title: Approval Workflow Automation
prompt_text: Act as a workflow automation specialist with expertise in process orchestration. Create an approval automation bot: 1) Request intake form (type/amount/justification), 2) Dynamic routing based on amount thresholds, 3) Approval notification to approver in Teams, 4) Reminder escalation if pending > [DAYS], 5) Status tracking and audit trail, 6) Notification to requester on decision. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Automates multi-level approval processes with tracking and escalation built in.
tips: Map your approval matrix (who approves what amounts). Use Power Automate flows for routing logic. Include an audit trail for compliance.

title: Customer Inquiry Router
prompt_text: Act as a customer experience specialist with expertise in service design. Build a customer inquiry routing bot: 1) Intent detection (billing/support/sales/returns), 2) Information gathering (account number/order ID/issue description), 3) Knowledge base search for self-service answers, 4) Handoff to live agent with context transfer, 5) Post-interaction survey. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Routes customer inquiries to the right team with pre-gathered context to reduce handle time.
tips: Use Topics for intent detection. Connect to Dynamics or Salesforce for customer lookup. Always provide a human handoff option — do not trap customers in bot loops.

title: Facilities Request Agent
prompt_text: Act as a conversational AI designer with expertise in enterprise bot development. Design a facilities request agent that: gathers request type (maintenance, space, amenities), location, urgency, and contact info. Categorizes and routes to facilities team. Provides status updates. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Automates facilities request intake and initial routing.
tips: Build request categorization logic; Copilot Studio will handle routing. Track requests in connected system.

title: Derived Field Calculation Engine
prompt_text: Act as a business rules analyst with expertise in calculated field design. I need derived fields added to this dataset. For each derived field below, create the calculation logic: [FIELD_LIST: e.g., Risk Score, Aging Days, Eligibility Status, Compliance Flag]. For each: (1) Business Definition — what does this field mean in plain language, (2) Calculation Formula — step-by-step logic including edge cases, (3) Input Dependencies — which source fields are required, (4) Edge Cases — what happens when inputs are NULL, zero, or conflicting, (5) Validation Rule — how to verify the calculation is correct (e.g., Risk Score must be 1-100, Aging cannot be negative), (6) Example Calculations — show 3 worked examples with different input scenarios. Format as a calculation specification table: Field | Formula | Inputs | Edge Case Handling | Validation Rule. Verify that all formulas handle NULL values gracefully and that no circular dependencies exist.
description: Designs derived field calculations with business rules, edge case handling, and validation logic.
tips: Provide real examples of input data for more accurate formulas. Specify the business rules driving each calculation. Add "Our risk scoring model uses [METHODOLOGY]" if you have an established framework.

title: Notes to Narrative Transformer
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer with expertise in transforming raw content into polished narratives. Convert these [INPUT_FORMAT: rough notes / bullet points / meeting scribbles / stream of consciousness] into [OUTPUT_FORMAT: polished paragraphs / formal narrative / structured document / presentation-ready content] for [AUDIENCE]. Transformation rules: (1) Preserve all factual content — do not add information that isn't in the source, (2) Organize logically — group related points, create clear paragraph structure, add transitions, (3) Fill implicit gaps — where notes use shorthand or assume context, expand to be clear to the target audience, (4) Maintain appropriate tone — [TONE: formal/conversational/technical/executive], (5) Add structure — headings, topic sentences, and concluding statements where appropriate. Keep the output to approximately [LENGTH] words. Flag any notes that are ambiguous or could be interpreted multiple ways — ask rather than assume. Verify that the narrative covers 100% of the source notes and that no content was fabricated.
description: Transforms rough notes and bullets into polished, audience-appropriate narratives while preserving all content.
tips: The messier the notes, the more context you should provide about the topic. Add "The most important points are [KEY_POINTS]" to guide emphasis. For technical content, add "The audience is [TECHNICAL/NON-TECHNICAL]" to calibrate jargon levels.

title: Legacy Content Modernizer
prompt_text: Act as a content strategist with expertise in content modernization and lifecycle management. This [CONTENT_TYPE: e.g., policy document, training material, webpage, procedure manual] was last updated [DATE] and needs modernization. Review and update: (1) Factual Accuracy — flag any information that is likely outdated (statistics, references, regulations, technology, org structure), (2) Language and Tone — update dated language, eliminate jargon that has fallen out of use, align with current [BRAND_VOICE/STYLE_GUIDE], (3) Structural Updates — improve formatting for modern consumption: shorter paragraphs, clear headings, scannable layout, mobile-friendly structure, (4) Inclusive Language — replace any non-inclusive terminology with current best practices, (5) Content Gaps — identify topics that should be covered in a modern version but aren't (e.g., remote work, AI tools, updated regulations), (6) Preservation Notes — content that should NOT be changed (legal language, regulatory citations, historical records). Track changes as: table: Section | Original | Updated | Reason for Change. Verify that the modernized version maintains the same regulatory authority and intent as the original.
description: Modernizes legacy content with factual updates, language refresh, inclusive language, and structural improvements.
tips: Provide the original document and your current style guide. Add "This document is referenced by [OTHER_DOCUMENTS]" to flag cross-reference dependencies. For regulatory content, add "Legal must approve changes to Sections [X]."

title: Data Freshness and Gap Detection
prompt_text: Act as a data governance analyst with expertise in data quality monitoring. Assess this dataset for freshness, completeness, and consistency: (1) Freshness Audit — for each data element, identify when it was last updated; flag anything older than [THRESHOLD: e.g., 30 days] as stale; categorize as Current/Aging/Stale/Expired, (2) Completeness Check — table: Field | Expected Records | Actual Records | Missing % | Business Impact (High/Medium/Low), (3) Consistency Scan — identify conflicting values across related fields (e.g., status says "Active" but termination date is populated), (4) Pattern Anomalies — flag unusual patterns: sudden drops in record counts, unexpected NULL spikes, values outside historical norms, (5) Remediation Plan — prioritize fixes by business impact: Issue | Severity | Data Owner | Recommended Action | Effort. Think through which gaps could cause downstream errors in reporting or decisions. Verify that your assessment covers all critical business fields, not just the ones with obvious issues.
description: Detects stale, missing, and inconsistent data with business impact assessment and remediation priorities.
tips: Define freshness thresholds by data type (e.g., financial data: 1 day, employee data: 7 days, market data: 30 days). Add "This data feeds [DOWNSTREAM_SYSTEM]" to help assess business impact of gaps.

title: Data Exception and Quality Flagger
prompt_text: Act as a data quality analyst with expertise in exception detection and validation. Review this dataset and systematically flag exceptions: (1) Missing Data — table: Field | Records Missing | % Missing | Business Impact | Recommended Default or Action, (2) Format Violations — values that don't match expected formats (dates, currencies, codes, enumerations), (3) Range Violations — numeric values outside expected bounds: Field | Value | Expected Range | Record ID, (4) Referential Integrity — values that don't match valid reference lists (e.g., department codes, employee IDs, GL accounts), (5) Business Rule Violations — logical inconsistencies: [SPECIFY_RULES: e.g., termination date cannot precede hire date, approved amount cannot exceed requested amount], (6) Cross-Field Validations — combinations that shouldn't coexist (e.g., status = Active but end date in the past). For each exception: assign severity (Critical / Warning / Info) and recommended resolution. Think through which exceptions could propagate errors downstream. Produce a summary: total records, records with exceptions, exception rate, and top 5 most common exception types.
description: Systematically flags data exceptions across multiple validation dimensions with severity ratings and resolution recommendations.
tips: Provide valid value lists and business rules for the most accurate validation. Add "Known data issues include [ISSUES]" to exclude known exceptions. For large datasets, add "Sample 10% for initial assessment then validate exceptions at scale."

title: Pre-Submission Completeness Audit
prompt_text: Act as a quality reviewer performing a final completeness check before submission. Review this [DELIVERABLE_TYPE: e.g., report, proposal, application, submission package] against [REQUIREMENTS: e.g., submission checklist, RFP requirements, regulatory filing requirements, internal standards]. Check: (1) Required Sections — table: Required Element | Present? | Complete? | Notes, (2) Formatting Compliance — page limits, font/size requirements, naming conventions, file format, (3) Content Quality — each section addresses what it's supposed to; no placeholder text, TBDs, or "[INSERT HERE]" markers remaining, (4) Supporting Documents — all referenced attachments, appendices, and exhibits are included and correctly numbered, (5) Internal Consistency — names, dates, numbers, and references are consistent throughout the document, (6) Final Polish — spelling, grammar, headers/footers correct, page numbers sequential, table of contents accurate. Produce a Go/No-Go recommendation. If No-Go: list the specific items that must be fixed before submission, ordered by severity. Verify your checklist by comparing against the submission requirements — do not rely on general best practices alone.
description: Performs final completeness audit on deliverables against submission requirements with Go/No-Go recommendation.
tips: Provide the submission requirements checklist. Add "Submission deadline is [DATE]" for urgency context. For multi-part submissions, add "Also verify cross-references between documents."

title: Benefits Program Review
prompt_text: Act as a total rewards analyst with expertise in compensation strategy and pay equity. Review our current benefits offering: [BENEFITS_SUMMARY]. Against peer companies and employee feedback, what's missing? What should we enhance? What can we reduce? Prioritize by impact and cost. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Optimizes benefits to meet employee needs within budget.
tips: Conduct employee survey on benefits preferences; Copilot will prioritize investments. Balance cost, utilization, and employee satisfaction.

title: Candidate Assessment Summary
prompt_text: Act as a senior talent acquisition strategist with expertise in inclusive hiring. I've interviewed [NUMBER] candidates for [ROLE]. Summary: [CANDIDATE_NOTES from multiple interviews]. Compare: fit for role, cultural fit, readiness, risk factors. Recommend top [NUMBER] for next round. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Synthesizes interview feedback to support hiring decisions.
tips: Use consistent notes from each interview; Copilot will compare fairly. Include any red flags or standout strengths from interviewers.

title: Headcount Analysis
prompt_text: Act as a senior talent acquisition strategist with expertise in inclusive hiring. [HEADCOUNT_DATA in Excel: employees by department, role, tenure, salary, location]. Summarize: headcount by org, turnover by department, cost per employee, any cost/headcount imbalances. Visualize trends. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Monitors workforce composition and efficiency metrics.
tips: Update monthly; Copilot will track trends. Use for budget and planning discussions.

title: Service Request Intake and Routing
prompt_text: Act as a service management specialist with expertise in request intake and classification. I have [NUMBER] incoming service requests (from [SOURCE: emails, forms, tickets, inbox]). Triage each request: (1) Classification — table: Request ID | Request Type (from [CATEGORIES]) | Sub-Type | Requester | Date Received, (2) Urgency Assessment — score each: Critical (business stopped) / High (significant impact, workaround exists) / Medium (inconvenience, can wait) / Low (enhancement, no time pressure) — with brief justification, (3) Routing — based on type and urgency, assign to: table: Request ID | Assigned Team | Assigned Individual (if determinable) | SLA Target, (4) Information Gaps — flag requests missing critical information needed to proceed, with specific questions to ask the requester, (5) Quick Wins — identify any requests that can be resolved immediately with a standard response. Think through whether any requests are duplicates or related to the same underlying issue. Verify that every request has been classified, prioritized, and routed.
description: Classifies, prioritizes, and routes incoming service requests with urgency assessment and information gap identification.
tips: Provide your category taxonomy and routing rules. Add "Requests from [VIP_GROUP] default to High priority" for organizational context. For email-based intake, include the subject line and first paragraph for each request.

title: Ticket Urgency and Category Classification
prompt_text: Act as a service desk analyst with expertise in ticket triage and categorization. Classify this batch of [NUMBER] tickets/requests. For each: (1) Category — assign to one of [CATEGORIES: e.g., Hardware, Software, Access, Network, HR, Facilities, Other], (2) Sub-Category — more specific classification within the category, (3) Urgency — Critical/High/Medium/Low based on: business impact, number of users affected, workaround availability, and time sensitivity, (4) Complexity — Simple (standard resolution exists) / Moderate (requires investigation) / Complex (requires specialist or multiple teams), (5) Suggested Response — for Simple items, draft a response or resolution; for Moderate/Complex, identify the next diagnostic step. Format as: table: Ticket # | Category | Sub-Cat | Urgency | Complexity | Routing | Suggested Next Step. Flag any tickets that appear to be duplicates, related incidents, or symptoms of a larger systemic issue. Verify that your urgency ratings are consistent across similar ticket types.
description: Classifies tickets by category, urgency, and complexity with suggested routing and responses.
tips: Provide your ticket category taxonomy for consistent classification. Add "Keywords that indicate Critical urgency include [KEYWORDS]" for automated classification rules. For recurring issues, add "Known issues include [LIST]" to speed resolution matching.

title: Structured Knowledge Extraction
prompt_text: Act as a knowledge extraction specialist with expertise in document analysis and structured data capture. Extract structured knowledge from this [DOCUMENT_TYPE: e.g., regulatory update, research report, meeting series, project retrospective]. Produce: (1) Key Themes — the 5-7 most important themes with supporting evidence and frequency of mention, (2) Obligations and Commitments — table: Obligation | Who | Deadline | Source Reference | Status (Active/Pending/Completed), (3) Decisions and Rationale — decisions made with the reasoning behind each, (4) Facts and Data Points — specific numbers, dates, benchmarks, and claims that should be preserved as reference data, (5) Open Questions — issues raised but not resolved, (6) Knowledge Taxonomy — organize extracted knowledge into categories that align with [OUR_TAXONOMY or suggest a logical taxonomy]. Think through what information has the longest shelf life vs. what is time-sensitive. Verify extraction completeness by cross-referencing your output against the document's table of contents or section headers.
description: Extracts structured knowledge from documents including themes, obligations, decisions, and data points.
tips: Specify the document type and your organizational knowledge taxonomy. Add "We need to track obligations from this document in [SYSTEM]" for integration-ready output. For regulatory documents, add "Flag any items with compliance deadlines."

title: Policy Q&A Engine
prompt_text: Act as a policy knowledge specialist with expertise in [POLICY_DOMAIN: e.g., HR, procurement, IT, compliance]. I have a question about our organizational policies: [QUESTION]. Using the policy documents available to you, provide: (1) Direct Answer — answer the question clearly and specifically in 2-3 sentences, (2) Policy Source — cite the specific policy, section, and effective date that governs this, (3) Key Rules — the relevant rules or requirements that apply, stated in plain language, (4) Common Exceptions — known exceptions or special circumstances that might change the answer, (5) Related Policies — other policies that may also be relevant to this situation, (6) Escalation — if the question involves judgment or interpretation, recommend who should make the final determination. If the policy is ambiguous or doesn't clearly address the question, say so — do not guess. Frame answers as "the policy states..." rather than giving personal advice. Verify that your answer reflects the most current version of the policy and note any recent changes.
description: Answers policy questions with source citations, plain-language explanations, and appropriate escalation guidance.
tips: Upload or reference the specific policy documents. Add "I am asking on behalf of a [ROLE] in [DEPARTMENT/LOCATION]" for context-appropriate answers. For complex questions, add "Also consider the intersection with [RELATED_POLICY]."

title: Institutional Knowledge Search
prompt_text: Act as a knowledge management specialist with expertise in organizational learning. I need to find information about [TOPIC] that may exist across our documents, reports, emails, and shared files. Search comprehensively and provide: (1) Direct Answers — what the available documents say about this topic, with source attribution, (2) Knowledge Map — table: Information Found | Source Document | Date | Author/Owner | Confidence (High/Medium/Low), (3) Conflicting Information — where sources disagree, with recommendation on which to trust, (4) Knowledge Gaps — what we probably SHOULD know about this topic but doesn't appear in available documents, (5) Subject Matter Experts — based on authorship and involvement, who in the organization likely has the deepest knowledge of this topic, (6) Related Topics — adjacent information that may be useful. Think through whether the available information is current and authoritative. Flag any documents that appear outdated or superseded. Verify that your search covered multiple document repositories, not just the most obvious one.
description: Searches across organizational knowledge sources with attribution, gap identification, and expert mapping.
tips: Describe the topic broadly first, then narrow. Add "I've already checked [SOURCES]" to avoid redundant searching. For time-sensitive topics, add "I need information from the last [PERIOD] only."

title: Categorize by Theme
prompt_text: Act as an executive assistant specialist with expertise in inbox management and prioritization. I have [NUMBER] emails about [GENERAL_TOPIC]. Organize by theme: what clusters of emails are there? What's the priority of each cluster? Any cross-cutting issues?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Identifies themes and priorities across email volume.
tips: Group by sender, topic, or urgency; Copilot will suggest useful categorizations. Use for inbox priority.

title: Weekly Digest
prompt_text: Act as an executive assistant specialist with expertise in inbox management and prioritization. Create a weekly email digest from my inbox [INBOX_SUMMARY]: what happened this week, what requires action, and what I should monitor. Format: brief, scannable, actionable. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Converts email volume into weekly briefing for communication partners.
tips: Aggregate themes and actions; Copilot will synthesize. Send at consistent time (Friday or Monday morning).

title: Auto-Organize
prompt_text: Act as an executive assistant specialist with expertise in inbox management and prioritization. This inbox has [NUMBER] emails across [TIMEFRAME]. Suggest filing/archiving strategy: what should stay in inbox? What should be archived? Create folder structure for [TOPIC_AREAS]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Designs email organization system for easier retrieval and review.
tips: Identify actionable vs. reference emails; archive reference. Create logical folder structure for quick filing.

title: Propose Responses
prompt_text: Act as an executive assistant specialist with expertise in inbox management and prioritization. From this batch of emails [EMAILS], propose responses: what should I say to each sender? Keep responses short and actionable. What needs escalation vs. what I can handle?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Jumpstarts email responses to clear inbox efficiently.
tips: Draft one response template per sender group; Copilot will suggest. Personalize before sending.

title: Identify Urgent
prompt_text: Act as an executive assistant specialist with expertise in inbox management and prioritization. From this email batch [EMAIL_SUMMARIES], flag urgent/escalated items: what requires immediate response? What can wait? What should I escalate? What can I delegate?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Surfaces time-sensitive issues in email volume.
tips: Look for keywords (urgent, escalated, overdue) and sender seniority; Copilot will flag. Review urgent items first.

title: Expiry and Renewal Monitor
prompt_text: Act as a lifecycle management specialist with expertise in compliance tracking. I have a dataset of [ENTITY_TYPE: e.g., contracts, licenses, certifications, visas, permits] with expiration dates. Build a monitoring report: (1) Urgency Dashboard — categorize every item: Expired (past due), Critical (expires within 30 days), Warning (31-90 days), Upcoming (91-180 days), Not Due (180+ days), (2) Critical Items Detail — table: Item | Owner | Expiry Date | Days Remaining | Renewal Lead Time Needed | Risk if Lapsed, (3) Renewal Pipeline — items requiring action this month and next month, with responsible party and estimated effort, (4) Historical Patterns — any items that have lapsed before or that consistently renew late, (5) Escalation Triggers — define when each urgency level should trigger a notification and to whom. Think through dependencies: are there items that cannot lapse without triggering cascading compliance issues? Verify that every item has an assigned owner and that no expired items are missing from the Critical list.
description: Monitors expiration dates across entities with urgency categorization, renewal pipeline, and escalation triggers.
tips: Include the renewal lead time for each item type (e.g., visa renewals take 6 months, contract renewals take 3 months). Add "Our compliance risk tolerance is [LEVEL]" to calibrate urgency thresholds.

title: Stalled Pipeline Detector
prompt_text: Act as a process efficiency analyst with expertise in workflow optimization. Review this pipeline/workflow data for [PROCESS_NAME: e.g., approvals, hiring, procurement, onboarding] and identify stalled items: (1) Aging Analysis — table: Item | Current Stage | Days in Stage | Expected SLA | Overdue By | Owner | Last Action Date, (2) Bottleneck Identification — which stage has the most items stuck and the longest average dwell time, (3) Root Cause Patterns — are stalls concentrated by: owner, department, request type, or time period, (4) Impact Assessment — what is the business cost of these delays (revenue impact, compliance risk, employee experience), (5) Recommended Actions — for each stalled item: specific next step, who should act, and suggested escalation if no action within [X] days. Flag any items where the assigned owner is no longer active (left the company, changed roles). Think through whether stalls are systemic (process design issue) vs. individual (person-specific bottleneck). Verify that your SLA calculations are based on business days, not calendar days.
description: Identifies stalled items in workflows with bottleneck analysis, root cause patterns, and escalation recommendations.
tips: Define your SLA expectations per stage (e.g., "Manager approval should take <3 business days"). Add "Exclude items intentionally on hold" to reduce noise. For large pipelines, focus on items >2x SLA first.

title: SLA Breach Identification
prompt_text: Act as a service delivery analyst with expertise in SLA management and compliance. Review performance against these SLAs for [SERVICE_OR_PROCESS]: [SLA_DEFINITIONS]. Produce: (1) SLA Compliance Dashboard — table: SLA Metric | Target | Actual | Status (Met/At Risk/Breached) | Trend (Improving/Stable/Declining), (2) Breaches Detail — for each breach: when it occurred, root cause, impact on customer/stakeholder, remediation taken, (3) At-Risk Items — SLAs currently within 10% of breach threshold, with predicted breach date if trend continues, (4) Pattern Analysis — are breaches concentrated in specific time periods, teams, or service types, (5) Improvement Recommendations — ranked by impact: Action | Expected SLA Improvement | Effort | Owner. Think through whether SLA definitions themselves need adjustment (are targets realistic given current capacity?). Verify that all SLA metrics have been assessed and that calculation methodology is consistent.
description: Identifies SLA breaches and at-risk metrics with pattern analysis and improvement recommendations.
tips: Provide both the SLA definitions and the actual performance data. Add "Business hours are [HOURS] in [TIMEZONE]" for time-based SLAs. For multi-tier SLAs, specify which tier applies to which customer segment.

title: Campaign Performance Deep Dive
prompt_text: Act as a performance marketing strategist with expertise in data-driven campaign optimization. Analyze campaign data for [CAMPAIGN]: 1) Performance Summary (impressions/clicks/conversions/revenue), 2) Channel Breakdown with efficiency metrics, 3) Audience Segment Performance, 4) Creative/Message Testing Results, 5) Funnel Drop-off Analysis, 6) Optimization Recommendations with projected impact. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Provides granular campaign analysis to optimize spend allocation and creative strategy.
tips: Include all touchpoint data for accurate attribution. Compare against benchmarks and prior campaigns. Test one variable at a time for clean learnings.

title: Brand Guidelines Review
prompt_text: Act as a brand strategist and communications expert. Review this brand asset [ASSET] against our guidelines: [BRAND_GUIDE_SUMMARY]. Does it align? Any tone, visual, or messaging issues? How should we fix it?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures brand consistency across touchpoints and reinforces identity.
tips: Use official brand guide; Copilot will spot deviations. Build templates for consistency across teams.

title: Contract Lifecycle Tracker
prompt_text: Act as a contract lifecycle manager with expertise in portfolio optimization. Track contract portfolio: 1) Summary (total contracts and spend and renewal schedule), 2) Contract Schedule (table: Vendor | Category | Value | Renewal Date | Status), 3) Upcoming Renewals flagged by priority, 4) Risk Assessment per vendor, 5) Negotiation Priorities ranked by value. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Single source of truth for contract renewals and negotiations with proactive deadline management.
tips: Export from contract management system (Icertis or Coupa) or maintain in Excel. Copilot will organize and highlight expiring contracts and flag risks.

title: Vendor Scorecard
prompt_text: Act as a strategic sourcing specialist with expertise in vendor performance management. [VENDOR_DATA in Excel: vendor names, performance metrics (quality, on-time delivery, responsiveness, innovation), and scores]. Summarize: top performers, underperformers, and performance trends. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Tracks vendor performance for continuous improvement and risk management.
tips: Update quarterly; Copilot will flag trends. Use for performance discussions and contract renewals.

title: Create from Document
prompt_text: Act as a presentation design strategist with expertise in executive storytelling. I have a document [DOCUMENT] about [TOPIC]. Create a [LENGTH]-slide presentation for [AUDIENCE] that: distills key insights, tells a compelling story, and drives [CALL_TO_ACTION]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Extracts narrative from long-form content for presentation format.
tips: Identify core story first; Copilot will map to slides. Remove jargon and detail; presentations are navigation aids, not documents.

title: Create from Transcript
prompt_text: Act as a presentation design strategist with expertise in executive storytelling. I have a transcript of [EVENT_TYPE]: [TRANSCRIPT]. Create a [LENGTH]-slide presentation for [AUDIENCE] that captures: key message, supporting evidence, and next steps. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Transforms spoken word into presentation narrative and structure.
tips: Identify main narrative and key quotes; Copilot will structure slides. Remove digressions and filler.

title: Restructure Presentation
prompt_text: Act as a presentation design strategist with expertise in executive storytelling. This presentation [CURRENT_STRUCTURE] needs restructuring for [NEW_AUDIENCE or GOAL]. How should we reorder? What emphasizes [KEY_MESSAGE]? What can we remove or combine?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Reorganizes existing content for better impact with new context or audience.
tips: Identify core narrative; Copilot will suggest better flow. Test new structure with audience to validate.

title: Speaker Notes
prompt_text: Act as a presentation design strategist with expertise in executive storytelling. Create speaker notes for this [LENGTH]-slide presentation on [TOPIC]. Each slide should have: main talking point, supporting detail, timing (~[DURATION] per slide), and transitions to next slide. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Provides presenter guidance for confident delivery.
tips: Write notes in conversational tone; Copilot will structure. Practice with notes to refine timing.

title: Update Existing Presentation
prompt_text: Act as a presentation design strategist with expertise in executive storytelling. This presentation [DECK_DETAILS] needs updating for [REASON: new data, changed situation, different audience]. What slides change? What new slides do we need? How to maintain flow?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Guides iterative presentation updates without starting from scratch.
tips: Identify what changed (data, audience, message) first; Copilot will suggest updates. Preserve core narrative.

title: Improve Visuals
prompt_text: Act as a presentation design strategist with expertise in executive storytelling. This presentation has [CURRENT_STATE: walls of text, dated visuals, poor charts]. Redesign for visual impact: cleaner layouts, better charts, compelling imagery, and clearer hierarchy. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Enhances visual design for engagement and clarity.
tips: Use white space; one idea per slide. Replace complex tables with clear charts; use visuals to support narrative.

title: Formal Letter and Correspondence Generator
prompt_text: Act as a professional correspondence writer with expertise in business and regulatory communication. Generate a formal [LETTER_TYPE: e.g., offer letter, termination notice, vendor notification, acknowledgment, reference, compliance notice] addressed to [RECIPIENT] regarding [SUBJECT]. Follow this template structure: (1) Header — sender organization, date, recipient name/title/address, (2) Subject Line — clear, specific, and reference any case/file numbers, (3) Opening — state the purpose in the first sentence; reference any prior correspondence, (4) Body — organized in logical paragraphs: context, key information, specific actions or decisions, (5) Obligations/Next Steps — what the recipient needs to do, by when, and consequences of non-action, (6) Closing — appropriate sign-off for the formality level, contact information for questions. Tone: [TONE: professional/empathetic/firm/neutral]. Comply with [APPLICABLE_REQUIREMENTS: e.g., employment standards act notice requirements, privacy legislation]. Verify that all legally required elements are present for this letter type. Flag any content that should be reviewed by Legal before sending.
description: Generates formal business correspondence from templates with compliance checks and legal flag reviews.
tips: Specify the letter type and any legal requirements. Add "Our letterhead template includes [ELEMENTS]" for brand consistency. For employment-related letters, always specify the jurisdiction for compliance requirements.

title: Draft from Notes
prompt_text: Act as a content operations specialist with expertise in editorial workflow management. I have notes from [EVENT_TYPE]: [NOTES]. Draft content about [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE]. Style: [STYLE]. Length: [LENGTH]. Capture the key insights and make it engaging. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Converts raw notes into publishable content assets efficiently.
tips: Organize notes by theme first; Copilot will draft. Review for accuracy and consistency.

title: Compare Content Versions
prompt_text: Act as a content operations specialist with expertise in editorial workflow management. Compare these [NUMBER] versions of [CONTENT]: [VERSION_DETAILS]. What's different? Which version is stronger and why? How should we merge or choose?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output.
description: Evaluates alternative drafts to select or combine the strongest elements.
tips: Score each on clarity, persuasiveness, and fit; Copilot will compare. Use for collaborative writing.

title: Consolidate Feedback
prompt_text: Act as a content operations specialist with expertise in editorial workflow management. I've gathered feedback on this draft [DRAFT] from [NUMBER] reviewers: [FEEDBACK_SUMMARY]. Consolidate feedback: common themes, conflicts, and recommendations. How should I revise?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Synthesizes multiple feedback inputs into coherent revision guidance.
tips: Categorize feedback (critical, important, nice-to-have); Copilot will prioritize. Resolve conflicts by asking for clarification.

title: Polish Final Draft
prompt_text: Act as a content operations specialist with expertise in editorial workflow management. Polish this [CONTENT_TYPE] for publication: review for flow, clarity, tone, impact, and brand alignment. Make it ready for [PUBLISHER/AUDIENCE]. Tighten by [%] without losing substance. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Final edit before publication to ensure quality and consistency.
tips: Review for typos, grammar, consistency; Copilot will polish. Have fresh eyes review before publishing.
