title: Publication Review Checklist
prompt_text: Act as a content operations specialist with expertise in editorial workflow management. This [CONTENT_TYPE] is ready for publication. Checklist: headline compelling? Message clear? Call-to-action present? Brand voice consistent? Links/images working? Any errors or issues?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Pre-publication quality and compliance check.
tips: Use a checklist to ensure consistency; Copilot will help. Get second review if high-stakes content.

title: Trend Identification
prompt_text: Act as a senior data analyst with expertise in business intelligence. Analyze this [DATA_TYPE]: [DATA or FILE_REFERENCE]. Identify trends over time (or across [DIMENSION]). For each trend: describe the direction, note the magnitude of change, and suggest a business implication. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Reveals directional patterns in data for decision-making and strategy.
tips: Provide dates or time periods if available; Copilot works better with context. For noisy data, ask for moving average or smoothed interpretation.

title: What-If Scenario Analysis
prompt_text: Act as a senior data analyst with expertise in business intelligence. Given this baseline [DATA/ASSUMPTION]: [INPUT], model what happens if [SCENARIO_A], [SCENARIO_B], and [SCENARIO_C]. Show impact on [KEY_METRIC] as: current state, scenario outcome, % change. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Explores decision impact without re-running complex models.
tips: Frame scenarios clearly: best case, worst case, most likely. Copilot will reason through cause-effect; review math for critical decisions.

title: Data Quality Assessment
prompt_text: Act as a senior data analyst with expertise in business intelligence. Assess the quality of this [DATA_TYPE]: [DATA]. Check for: completeness (% missing), consistency (format, values), accuracy (obvious errors), and timeliness (age of data). Rate overall quality and suggest cleanup priorities. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Determines fitness of data for decision-making and identifies remediation needs.
tips: Provide context about the data source and use case; Copilot will prioritize quality issues. Useful before relying on data for major decisions.

title: Comparison Across Groups
prompt_text: Act as a senior data analyst with expertise in business intelligence. Compare [METRIC] across [DIMENSION]: [VALUES]. Show performance of each group: absolute value, rank, and performance vs. average. Highlight winners, laggards, and why the gaps might exist. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output.
description: Benchmarks performance within and between groups for accountability and learning.
tips: Provide context about each group (geography, team, product); Copilot will suggest relevant comparisons. Useful for dashboard design and team scorecards.

title: Forecasting and Extrapolation
prompt_text: Act as a senior data analyst with expertise in business intelligence. Given this historical [DATA_TYPE] from [TIME_PERIOD]: [DATA], forecast [METRIC] for [FUTURE_PERIOD]. Note assumptions, confidence level, and key drivers of the forecast. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output.
description: Projects future performance based on historical patterns and trends.
tips: Copilot can extrapolate trends but should not be your primary forecasting tool for critical decisions. Use this for directional planning; validate with statistical models.

title: Segmentation and Clustering
prompt_text: Act as a senior data analyst with expertise in business intelligence. Segment this [DATA_TYPE] by [DIMENSION]. Identify natural clusters or groupings based on [CRITERIA]. For each segment: size, key characteristics, and business relevance or action. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Reveals distinct groups within a population for targeted strategy.
tips: Copilot will suggest reasonable segments; validate against your business model. Useful for targeting, messaging, and resource allocation.

title: Root Cause Analysis
prompt_text: Act as a senior data analyst with expertise in business intelligence. We're seeing [SYMPTOM/PROBLEM] in [METRIC]. Walk through the factors that could cause this: [DIMENSIONS or FACTORS]. Which factors changed recently? Which are most likely culprits?. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Diagnoses the drivers behind business problems for targeted solutions.
tips: Use 5-Whys or fishbone structure; ask Copilot to walk through each branch. Review findings against recent changes (system, process, market) for validation.

title: Dashboard Design Recommendation
prompt_text: Act as a senior data analyst with expertise in business intelligence. For this [USE_CASE/AUDIENCE], recommend a dashboard structure. What [NUMBER] metrics matter most? How should they be organized? What granularity (daily, weekly, monthly)? Suggest 2-3 visualizations. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Blueprints effective dashboards aligned with user needs and decision workflows.
tips: Describe the user (what decisions they make, what they check weekly); Copilot will suggest relevant metrics. Review for actionability and timeliness.

title: Data Quality Assessment
prompt_text: Act as a data management specialist with expertise in data quality and governance. Assess data quality for [DATASET_OR_SYSTEM]: 1) Completeness (% of missing values by field), 2) Accuracy (known errors or inconsistencies), 3) Timeliness (data freshness and update frequency), 4) Consistency (cross-system alignment), 5) Recommendations (prioritized fixes with effort estimate). Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Provides a structured data quality health check to inform cleanup priorities and data governance.
tips: Provide sample data or describe the schema. Copilot can analyze patterns and flag anomalies. Pair with a data steward for remediation planning.

title: Dashboard Data Preparation
prompt_text: Act as a data management specialist with expertise in data quality and governance. Prepare this raw data for dashboard visualization: 1) Clean and standardize column formats, 2) Remove duplicates and handle missing values, 3) Create calculated columns ([SPECIFY_METRICS]), 4) Build pivot table summaries, 5) Add conditional formatting to highlight outliers, 6) Create a summary sheet with key KPIs. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Transforms raw data into dashboard-ready format with calculations and formatting.
tips: Describe the final dashboard you want. Copilot will work backwards to structure the data correctly. Keep raw data on a separate sheet from calculations.

title: Budget vs Actual Tracker
prompt_text: Act as a financial analyst with expertise in modeling and variance analysis. Create a budget variance tracker: 1) Monthly columns (Jan-Dec) with Budget and Actual and Variance rows, 2) Conditional formatting (green for favorable and red for unfavorable variance), 3) YTD rollup formulas, 4) Variance % calculation, 5) Chart showing budget vs. actual trend line, 6) Commentary column for explanations. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Automates budget tracking with visual variance highlighting and trend analysis.
tips: Enter budget figures first then actuals monthly. Copilot will calculate variances and flag items exceeding threshold. Set a materiality threshold (e.g. 5%).

title: Sales Pipeline Analyzer
prompt_text: Act as a sales operations analyst with expertise in pipeline management. Analyze this sales pipeline data: 1) Pipeline Summary (table: Stage | Count | Value | Avg Deal Size | Conversion Rate), 2) Weighted Pipeline Value, 3) Aging Analysis (deals stuck in stage > [DAYS]), 4) Win/Loss Analysis by [SEGMENT], 5) Forecast for next quarter, 6) Rep Performance Comparison. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Provides pipeline health metrics and forecasting from raw CRM export data.
tips: Export from CRM as CSV. Copilot will calculate stage conversion rates and weighted values. Flag deals with no activity in 30+ days.

title: Inventory Management Tracker
prompt_text: Act as an operations excellence specialist with expertise in process optimization. Create an inventory tracking workbook: 1) Item Master (SKU | Description | Category | Unit Cost | Reorder Point), 2) Stock Levels (current quantity and location), 3) Movement Log (date | item | quantity | type: in/out), 4) Alerts for items below reorder point, 5) ABC Analysis (classify by value), 6) Turnover Rate Calculation. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Automates inventory tracking with reorder alerts and classification analysis.
tips: Set reorder points based on lead time and demand variability. Copilot will calculate turnover and classify items. Review ABC classification quarterly.

title: Project Resource Allocation
prompt_text: Act as a certified project manager with expertise in stakeholder communication. Build a resource allocation model: 1) Team Roster (name | role | availability % | cost rate), 2) Project List (project | phase | start | end | required skills), 3) Allocation Matrix (who is assigned where and at what %), 4) Utilization Dashboard (actual vs. capacity), 5) Conflict Detection (over-allocated resources highlighted). Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Visualizes resource allocation across projects to prevent overcommitment and identify capacity gaps.
tips: Update weekly for accuracy. Flag anyone consistently above 90% utilization. Include planned PTO and training time in capacity calculations.

title: Operational Efficiency Metrics
prompt_text: Act as a senior analytics specialist with expertise in data-driven decision making. Analyze operational efficiency in [DATASET]: 1) Throughput Metrics (volume/time/cost per unit), 2) Bottleneck Identification, 3) Capacity Utilization, 4) Quality Metrics (error rate/rework/defects), 5) Benchmark Comparison (internal teams or industry), 6) Improvement Opportunities ranked by ROI. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Identifies operational inefficiencies and quantifies improvement opportunities from process data.
tips: Include timestamps for process steps to identify bottlenecks. Compare across teams or time periods. Focus on the constraint — improving non-bottlenecks does not help.

title: Employee Analytics & Workforce Insights
prompt_text: Act as a people analytics specialist with expertise in workforce insights. Analyze workforce data in [DATASET]: 1) Headcount Overview (by department/level/tenure), 2) Turnover Analysis (voluntary vs. involuntary and by segment), 3) Diversity Metrics, 4) Compensation Distribution, 5) Engagement Score Correlation Analysis, 6) Flight Risk Indicators. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Provides people analytics insights to inform HR strategy and workforce planning decisions.
tips: Anonymize data before analysis. Look for correlations between engagement scores and turnover. Flag teams with turnover 2x company average.

title: Survey Results Analyzer
prompt_text: Act as a senior analytics specialist with expertise in data-driven decision making. Analyze survey results in [DATASET]: 1) Response Rate and demographics, 2) Score Summary (table: Question | Mean | Median | Distribution), 3) Top Scores and Bottom Scores, 4) Open-Text Theme Analysis, 5) Segment Comparison (by department/tenure/role), 6) Year-over-Year Trends, 7) Priority Actions based on impact analysis. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Transforms raw survey data into actionable insights with statistical rigor and thematic analysis.
tips: Include demographic fields for segmentation. Copilot will calculate statistical significance for group differences. Focus actions on items with high importance and low scores.

title: Content Drafting from Notes
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer and communications strategist. Using these notes: [NOTES], draft a [DOCUMENT_TYPE] for [AUDIENCE] about [TOPIC]. Organize by [STRUCTURE], use [TONE], and ensure it answers [KEY_QUESTION]. Include a clear call-to-action. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Transforms raw notes into polished deliverables for communication and alignment.
tips: Start with a clear structure in mind (e.g., problem-solution-next-steps); Copilot will follow it. Specify tone explicitly: formal, conversational, technical, etc.

title: Multi-Source Content Synthesis
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer and communications strategist. I have [NUMBER] sources covering [TOPIC]. Synthesize them into a [DOCUMENT_TYPE] for [AUDIENCE] that resolves contradictions, highlights consensus, and provides actionable insight. Use an [OUTLINE_STRUCTURE]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Merges research and inputs into cohesive narratives for reports and briefs.
tips: Paste or name each source; Copilot tracks provenance. Useful for white papers, market briefs, and strategy documents.

title: Refinement and Polish
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer and communications strategist. Review this [DOCUMENT_TYPE] for: flow, clarity, redundancy, and impact. Revise for a [SENIORITY_LEVEL] audience, tighten by [PERCENTAGE]%, and improve visual hierarchy. Preserve all content intent. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Enhances existing drafts for publication readiness and stakeholder reception.
tips: Copilot is good at tightening; specify the % reduction you need. For very long docs, ask for section-by-section feedback first.

title: Tone and Voice Adjustment
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer and communications strategist. This [DOCUMENT_TYPE] is currently [CURRENT_TONE]. Rewrite it in [TARGET_TONE] while keeping all information intact. The audience is [AUDIENCE] and the use case is [USE_CASE]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Adapts messaging to audience expectations without losing substance.
tips: Give examples of the target tone if possible; Copilot will match style better. Useful for executive summaries (from technical docs) or vice versa.

title: Audience Adaptation
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer and communications strategist. Adapt this [DOCUMENT_TYPE] for [TARGET_AUDIENCE] (currently written for [ORIGINAL_AUDIENCE]). Adjust: technical depth, terminology, examples, and emphasis to match [AUDIENCE_NEED/CONCERN]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Reframes messaging for different stakeholder groups from a single draft.
tips: Describe what the new audience cares about (e.g., CFO wants ROI, ops wants feasibility); Copilot will emphasize accordingly. Useful for multi-stakeholder rollouts.

title: Version Merging and Reconciliation
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer and communications strategist. I have [NUMBER] versions of [DOCUMENT_TYPE] from different authors. Merge them into one coherent document that incorporates all contributions. Preserve distinct insights and flag conflicting statements. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Consolidates collaborative drafts without losing authorial input.
tips: List authors so Copilot can track perspectives; useful for pre-review merges. Ask for a reconciliation table if conflicts are significant.

title: Outline Expansion
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer and communications strategist. I have a [DOCUMENT_TYPE] outline: [OUTLINE]. Expand each section with 2-3 paragraphs of substantial content on [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE]. Use [TONE] and include [SPECIFIC_ELEMENTS]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Turns skeletal outlines into first-draft prose for faster authoring.
tips: Provide an outline structure and key talking points; Copilot will flesh out. Review the output for accuracy in technical sections.

title: Brand Voice Consistency Check
prompt_text: Act as a professional writer and communications strategist. Review this [DOCUMENT_TYPE] against our brand guidelines: [GUIDELINES_SUMMARY]. Adjust language, tone, examples, and messaging to align with [BRAND_ATTRIBUTE]. Maintain all factual content. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures marketing and corporate messaging reflects brand identity consistently.
tips: Provide brand guidelines, brand book excerpts, or past examples; Copilot will learn your voice. Useful for multi-author communications.

title: Change Management Communication Plan
prompt_text: Act as a change management specialist with expertise in organizational transformation. I am rolling out [CHANGE_INITIATIVE] affecting [AUDIENCE]. Draft a change management communication plan with: 1) Stakeholder Map (who needs to know what when), 2) Key Messages per audience tier, 3) Communication Timeline (pre-launch / launch week / post-launch), 4) FAQ document for managers, 5) Resistance mitigation talking points. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Creates structured change comms that address different stakeholder needs and proactively manage resistance.
tips: Provide the change details and impacted groups. Copilot will tailor messaging by audience. Include the business rationale — people accept change better when they understand why.

title: Root Cause Analysis Framework
prompt_text: Act as a process improvement specialist with expertise in root cause analysis. I have encountered [PROBLEM_DESCRIPTION]. Walk me through a root cause analysis: 1) Symptom Documentation, 2) Timeline (when did it start and what changed), 3) Scope (what is affected vs. what is not), 4) Top 3 theories with diagnostic questions for each, 5) Most likely root cause with evidence, 6) Recommended fixes ranked by effort vs. impact. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Structured problem-solving that prevents treating symptoms instead of addressing actual causes.
tips: Describe symptoms in specific detail. Copilot will ask probing questions. Resist jumping to solutions before validating root cause.

title: Stakeholder Alignment Document
prompt_text: Act as a strategy consultant with expertise in business planning and stakeholder alignment. I need to align [STAKEHOLDERS] on [INITIATIVE]. Create a one-page alignment document with: 1) Problem Statement (2-3 sentences), 2) Proposed Solution, 3) Expected Outcomes with metrics, 4) Resource Requirements, 5) Timeline and milestones, 6) Decision needed and by when. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures all stakeholders have the same understanding of what is being proposed and what decision is required.
tips: Keep it to one page. Decision-makers scan — use bold headers and keep each section to 2-3 sentences max. End with a clear ask.

title: Process Documentation Template
prompt_text: Act as an operations excellence specialist with expertise in process optimization. Document the process for [PROCESS_NAME]: 1) Process Owner and Stakeholders, 2) Trigger (what starts this process), 3) Step-by-step procedure with decision points, 4) Roles and Responsibilities (RACI), 5) Systems and Tools used, 6) Exceptions and escalation paths, 7) SLA and metrics. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Captures institutional knowledge into reusable process documentation that survives team changes.
tips: Walk through the process mentally before documenting. Flag any steps where tribal knowledge is needed. Include screenshots or references where helpful.

title: Vendor Evaluation Scorecard
prompt_text: Act as a senior specialist with deep domain expertise. Evaluate [VENDORS] for [REQUIREMENT]. Create a weighted scorecard: 1) Evaluation Criteria (table: Criterion | Weight | Vendor A | Vendor B | Vendor C), 2) Scoring methodology, 3) Strengths and weaknesses per vendor, 4) Total weighted score, 5) Recommendation with rationale. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output.
description: Structured vendor comparison that makes procurement decisions transparent and defensible.
tips: Define criteria and weights before scoring. Include both quantitative (price and SLA) and qualitative (innovation and cultural fit) factors.

title: Workshop Facilitation Guide
prompt_text: Act as a workshop facilitator with expertise in collaborative problem-solving. Design a [DURATION] workshop on [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE]. Include: 1) Objectives and success metrics, 2) Agenda with timing (table: Time | Activity | Method | Materials), 3) Discussion prompts for each segment, 4) Breakout group instructions, 5) Parking lot template, 6) Follow-up action plan template. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Creates a ready-to-run workshop plan with timing and discussion prompts and facilitation notes.
tips: Test timing by running through agenda mentally. Build in 10% buffer time. Have a backup activity if discussion runs short. Always end with clear next steps.

title: Knowledge Transfer Checklist
prompt_text: Act as a knowledge management specialist with expertise in organizational learning. Create a knowledge transfer plan for [DEPARTING_ROLE]: 1) Critical Knowledge Inventory (what only this person knows), 2) Document List (what needs to be written down), 3) Handoff Sessions (table: Topic | Recipient | Date | Status), 4) Access and Credentials Transfer, 5) Relationship Map (key contacts and context), 6) Open Items and Risks. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures institutional knowledge is captured before team transitions to prevent operational gaps.
tips: Start knowledge transfer 4-6 weeks before departure. Prioritize undocumented tribal knowledge. Have the departing person review the final document.

title: Policy Exception Request
prompt_text: Act as a governance and compliance specialist with expertise in policy management. Draft a policy exception request for [POLICY_NAME]: 1) Current Policy Statement, 2) Requested Exception (what and why and for how long), 3) Business Justification with impact if denied, 4) Risk Assessment and proposed mitigations, 5) Approval Chain and timeline needed. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output.
description: Creates a well-structured exception request that balances business need with risk management.
tips: Include the specific policy clause number. Quantify the business impact. Propose a time-bound exception with review date rather than permanent change.

title: Executive Briefing One-Pager
prompt_text: Act as a senior executive communications writer who specializes in leadership-ready content. Create an executive one-pager on [TOPIC]: Structure as: Header (title and date and author), Situation (2-3 sentences), Complication (what changed or what is the challenge), Resolution (recommended path), Key Metrics (small table), Next Steps and Ask. Keep under 500 words. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Distills complex topics into the concise format executives actually read and act on.
tips: Use the SCR framework (Situation Complication Resolution). Lead with the ask not the background. Executives decide — give them what they need to decide.

title: Multi-Step Analysis Chain
prompt_text: Act as a senior analyst with expertise in multi-step analytical frameworks. Step 1: Analyze [DATA_SET] for [KEY_METRICS] — summarize current performance. Step 2: Identify trends across [TIME_PERIOD] — flag inflection points. Step 3: Benchmark against [COMPARISON_BASELINE]. Step 4: Forecast [METRIC] for next [PERIOD] using trend data. Format each step as a separate section with findings and a confidence rating. Think through each element systematically before producing output.
description: Breaks complex analysis into sequential notebook cells for deep investigation combining data analysis and trend detection and forecasting.
tips: Works well with structured data tables. Use Copy-Paste from Excel to bring data into Copilot. Each step builds on previous findings. Add your own data sources inline.

title: Research Synthesis Deep Dive
prompt_text: Act as a research analyst with expertise in evidence-based synthesis. Synthesize [NUMBER] research sources on [TOPIC]. Structure: 1) Key Findings (table format), 2) Consensus Points across sources, 3) Areas of Disagreement (with source attribution), 4) Knowledge Gaps, 5) Recommended Next Steps. Cite each source by name and date. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Consolidates diverse research into a single source of truth with clear differentiation between consensus and outliers.
tips: Paste abstracts or short excerpts and label each source (e.g. McKinsey 2024 or Gartner 2023). Copilot works best when sources are clearly separated.

title: Competitive Intelligence Brief
prompt_text: Act as a senior analyst with expertise in multi-step analytical frameworks. Build a competitive landscape analysis for [COMPETITORS]. Create a matrix with columns: Competitor | Key Strengths | Weaknesses | Market Position | Pricing Approach | Innovation Areas. Add sections for: Market Trends, Threats to Our Position, and Strategic Opportunities. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Consolidates competitive landscape into a single living document that can be regularly updated as market conditions change.
tips: Paste in recent press releases or earnings call summaries or website data. Copilot will parse and structure into matrix. Keep source links for future reference.

title: Compliance & Risk Review
prompt_text: Act as a governance and compliance specialist with expertise in policy management. Review [DOCUMENT_TYPE or REGULATION] for compliance gaps. Structure: 1) Requirement Summary (table: Requirement | Current Status | Gap | Mitigation), 2) Priority Risks ranked High/Medium/Low, 3) Remediation Plan with owners, 4) Implementation Timeline with milestones. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures regulatory and internal policy adherence with clear tracking of remediation actions and accountability.
tips: Include regulation titles and version dates. Copilot flags missing clauses. Useful for SOC2 GDPR and internal policy reviews.

title: Training Program Design
prompt_text: Act as a learning and development strategist with expertise in capability building. Design a training program on [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE]. Structure: 1) Learning Objectives by level (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced), 2) Module Breakdown (table: Module | Duration | Key Concepts | Assessment Method), 3) Resource Links, 4) Success Metrics and KPIs. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Creates structured learning programs that scale across teams and track progress against knowledge gaps.
tips: Define target skill level and time constraints upfront. Copilot adapts depth accordingly. Include links to internal wikis or external resources as reference materials.

title: Customer Journey Mapping
prompt_text: Act as a senior specialist with deep domain expertise. Map the customer journey for [PRODUCT_OR_SERVICE]: 1) Touchpoints (table: Stage | Interaction | Channel | Sentiment), 2) Pain Points by Stage, 3) Moments of Truth, 4) Improvement Opportunities ranked by impact, 5) Owner and Timeline for each improvement. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Visualizes end-to-end customer experience and identifies high-impact improvement areas for product and service teams.
tips: Use customer feedback and support ticket data and user research. Copilot will synthesize into journey map. Flag emotional highs and lows for prioritization.

title: SWOT & Strategic Positioning
prompt_text: Act as a strategy consultant with expertise in business planning and stakeholder alignment. Conduct a SWOT analysis for [ENTITY_OR_INITIATIVE]: Build a 4-quadrant matrix (Strengths | Weaknesses | Opportunities | Threats) with 5-7 items per quadrant. Synthesize into: 1) Key Strategic Themes, 2) Priority Actions, 3) Required Capabilities and Resources. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Structured strategic assessment that feeds into planning and resource allocation decisions.
tips: Involve 2-3 stakeholders in the SWOT brainstorm and paste their inputs. Copilot consolidates and identifies strategic priorities. Output drives roadmap.

title: Email Drafting
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. Draft a professional email about [TOPIC] to [RECIPIENT/ROLE]. Purpose: [PURPOSE]. Tone: [TONE]. Include: [KEY_POINTS]. Keep it to [SENTENCE_COUNT] sentences. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Jumpstarts email writing for busy professionals.
tips: Specify purpose clearly (inform, request, escalate); Copilot will adjust tone. Useful for high-stakes emails; always review before sending.

title: Meeting Preparation
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. I have a meeting with [ATTENDEES] about [TOPIC]. Prepare: key questions to ask, talking points, and success criteria. Context: [BACKGROUND]. What should I have ready?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures productive meetings with clear objectives and prepared inputs.
tips: Describe your goals and any known tensions; Copilot will suggest discussion paths. Useful for 1:1s, negotiations, and leadership meetings.

title: Decision Matrix
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. We need to decide between [OPTION_A], [OPTION_B], and [OPTION_C] on [DECISION_TOPIC]. Create a decision matrix: criteria (e.g., cost, timeline, risk), score each option, weight criteria, and recommend. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Structures decision-making for teams and reduces analysis paralysis.
tips: Define criteria that matter (financial, strategic, operational) upfront; Copilot will score. Useful for procurement, vendor selection, and strategy choices.

title: Presentation Outline
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. Create a presentation outline for [TOPIC] to [AUDIENCE]. Goal: [GOAL]. Format: [SLIDE_STRUCTURE]. Include: opening hook, [NUMBER] key messages, supporting evidence, and close. Time: [DURATION]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Scaffolds presentations from concept to detailed outline.
tips: Provide context about audience seniority and familiarity with topic; Copilot will adjust depth. Use this before opening PowerPoint.

title: Stakeholder Communication Plan
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. For this initiative [DESCRIPTION], create a communication plan. Who needs to know? What message for each stakeholder? When and how to communicate? Format as: Stakeholder | Message | Channel | Timing. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures alignment and reduces surprises during change initiatives.
tips: List stakeholders by type (impacted, decision-maker, supporter, resistant); Copilot will suggest tailored messages. Useful for organizational changes.

title: Weekly Digest Creation
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. Create a weekly digest for [AUDIENCE] covering: [TOPICS]. Include: what happened this week, what matters, what to watch, and ask (what do you need?). Keep to [LENGTH]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Reduces email volume and improves team alignment on priorities.
tips: Collect inputs from team; Copilot will synthesize. Send on a consistent day/time for habit formation. Useful for cross-functional teams.

title: Feedback Synthesis
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. I've gathered feedback from [NUMBER] people on [TOPIC]. Synthesize: common themes, areas of consensus, key concerns, and recommendations. Group feedback by [DIMENSION]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Identifies actionable insights from feedback and surveys.
tips: Share representative quotes; Copilot will categorize. Useful for product, service, and culture feedback review.

title: Constraint Analysis
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. We're trying to [GOAL] but facing constraints: [CONSTRAINTS]. Analyze each constraint: is it real or perceived, how could we work around it, and what are the trade-offs? Rank by impact. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Challenges assumptions and uncovers hidden opportunities in constrained situations.
tips: Distinguish between policy constraints and hard limits; Copilot will focus on addressable ones. Useful for creative problem-solving sessions.

title: OKR Framework Builder
prompt_text: Act as a strategic planning specialist with expertise in OKR frameworks. Help me build OKRs for [TEAM_OR_INITIATIVE] for [PERIOD]. For each Objective: 1) Objective statement (qualitative and inspiring), 2) 3-5 Key Results (quantitative and measurable), 3) Initiatives that drive each KR, 4) Owner and timeline. Ensure OKRs are ambitious but achievable. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Creates well-structured OKRs that connect team work to strategic priorities with clear measurement.
tips: Start with 2-3 objectives max. Key Results should be outcomes not activities. Use the formula: Verb + Metric + From X to Y by Date.

title: Decision Log & RACI Matrix
prompt_text: Act as a decision science specialist with expertise in structured frameworks. Create a decision framework for [PROJECT_OR_INITIATIVE]: 1) Decision Log (table: Decision | Date | Made By | Rationale | Status), 2) RACI Matrix (table: Activity | Responsible | Accountable | Consulted | Informed), 3) Escalation Path for unresolved decisions, 4) Review cadence. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output.
description: Brings structure to decision-making and accountability across complex projects.
tips: Define RACI before kickoff. Only one person should be Accountable per row. Review the decision log monthly to ensure consistency.

title: Industry Trend Analysis
prompt_text: Act as a research analyst with expertise in evidence-based synthesis. Research current trends in [INDUSTRY] for [YEAR]. Structure findings as: 1) Top 5 Trends with evidence, 2) Market Size and Growth Projections, 3) Key Players and their strategies, 4) Emerging Technologies or Disruptions, 5) Regulatory Changes on the horizon, 6) Implications for [OUR_COMPANY]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Provides a structured industry overview for strategic planning and competitive awareness.
tips: Specify geographic scope and time horizon. Copilot searches across multiple sources. Cross-reference findings — no single source tells the full story.

title: Best Practice Research Brief
prompt_text: Act as a research analyst with expertise in evidence-based synthesis. Research best practices for [TOPIC] across [INDUSTRY_OR_FUNCTION]. Deliver: 1) Summary of leading approaches (table: Organization | Approach | Key Features | Results), 2) Common Success Factors, 3) Common Pitfalls, 4) Applicability Assessment for our context, 5) Recommended adoption roadmap. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Synthesizes external best practices into actionable recommendations tailored to your context.
tips: Specify what success looks like for your organization. Copilot will filter practices by relevance. Include both aspirational leaders and comparable peers.

title: Regulatory Landscape Scan
prompt_text: Act as a regulatory compliance specialist with expertise in risk assessment. Scan the regulatory landscape for [INDUSTRY_OR_DOMAIN] in [GEOGRAPHY]: 1) Current Regulations (table: Regulation | Scope | Key Requirements | Deadline), 2) Proposed/Pending Changes, 3) Enforcement Trends, 4) Peer Company Compliance Approaches, 5) Risk Assessment for our operations, 6) Recommended preparation steps. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Maps the regulatory environment to support compliance planning and risk assessment.
tips: Specify jurisdictions that matter. Copilot will search government sources and industry publications. Flag any deadlines within 12 months as urgent.

title: Technology Evaluation Research
prompt_text: Act as a technology evaluation specialist with expertise in enterprise architecture. Research [TECHNOLOGY_CATEGORY] solutions: 1) Market Overview and maturity, 2) Top Solutions Comparison (table: Solution | Strengths | Weaknesses | Pricing | Best For), 3) Analyst Ratings (Gartner/Forrester if available), 4) Customer Sentiment Summary, 5) Integration Considerations for our stack, 6) Recommendation. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Provides a comprehensive technology landscape assessment for procurement and architecture decisions.
tips: Specify must-have requirements and current technology stack. Copilot will filter and compare. Include total cost of ownership not just license fees.

title: Customer Sentiment Analysis
prompt_text: Act as a competitive intelligence analyst with expertise in market dynamics. Research customer sentiment for [BRAND_OR_PRODUCT]: 1) Overall Sentiment Score and trend, 2) Positive Themes (what customers love), 3) Negative Themes (common complaints), 4) Competitive Sentiment Comparison, 5) Channel-by-Channel Breakdown (social/review sites/forums), 6) Recommended response strategy. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Aggregates customer voice data from multiple channels into actionable sentiment insights.
tips: Specify review platforms and social channels to monitor. Copilot will scan and categorize. Look for sentiment shifts over time not just point-in-time.

title: Academic Research Summary
prompt_text: Act as a research analyst with expertise in evidence-based synthesis. Summarize academic research on [TOPIC]: 1) Key Studies (table: Author/Year | Finding | Methodology | Sample Size), 2) Consensus Findings, 3) Methodological Limitations, 4) Practical Implications for [CONTEXT], 5) Gaps Requiring Further Research, 6) Recommended reading list. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Distills academic literature into practical business insights with proper source attribution.
tips: Specify the time period and key journals or databases. Copilot will summarize abstracts and findings. Note study limitations — not all research is equal.

title: Startup & Innovation Landscape Scan
prompt_text: Act as an innovation strategist with expertise in emerging technologies and business models. Map the startup ecosystem for [DOMAIN]: 1) Emerging Players (table: Company | Founded | Funding | Focus | Stage), 2) Technology Approaches, 3) Funding Trends, 4) Partnership Opportunities, 5) Potential Threats to our business, 6) Recommended engagement strategy. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Identifies startup activity and innovation trends relevant to your strategic interests.
tips: Specify what makes a startup relevant (technology/market/capability). Copilot will scan Crunchbase and TechCrunch and industry sources. Update quarterly.

title: Theme Detection Across Documents
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. Scan these [NUMBER] documents and identify recurring themes, patterns, or concerns. Group by theme and note how many documents mention each. Highlight any themes that contradict each other. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Reveals patterns and consensus across multiple inputs for insight discovery.
tips: Paste short excerpts or document names with brief context; Copilot may miss nuance in very long inputs. Works best with 3-10 sources.

title: Risk and Issue Identification
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. Review this [DOCUMENT_TYPE] and flag any risks, issues, or red flags. For each, rate Severity (High/Medium/Low) and Likelihood (High/Medium/Low), and suggest a mitigation or next step. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Proactively surfaces business risks embedded in documents or conversations.
tips: Provide context about your risk appetite; Copilot will calibrate severity ratings. Be explicit about what "risk" means in your domain (financial, reputational, operational).

title: Comparison and Delta Analysis
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. Compare these two [DOCUMENT_TYPE]s and show me the deltas: what's new, what's removed, what's changed. Format as three columns: Added | Removed | Modified. Highlight material differences only. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Quickly identifies what has changed between versions for review and approval workflows.
tips: Copilot handles version-to-version well; for before/after narratives, be explicit about which is which. Use this before signing off on updates.

title: Stakeholder Position Mapping
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. From this [DOCUMENT_TYPE or MEETING_TRANSCRIPT], map stakeholder positions: who supports what, who has concerns, and where is consensus? Format as a table: Stakeholder | Position | Concerns | Influence Level. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Visualizes organizational alignment and identifies where negotiation is needed.
tips: Name stakeholders explicitly if possible; Copilot will anchor on real names better than pronouns. Useful for pre-meeting planning.

title: Document Consolidation
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. I have [NUMBER] related documents about [TOPIC]. Consolidate them into one coherent brief, removing duplicates, reconciling conflicting info, and organizing by [SECTION_STRUCTURE]. Call out any unresolved contradictions. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Merges multiple inputs into a single source of truth for distributed teams.
tips: Provide a suggested structure upfront; Copilot will follow it more precisely. Name the documents so you can refer back to source.

title: Metrics and KPI Extraction
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. Extract all metrics, KPIs, and numerical targets from this [DOCUMENT_TYPE]. Create a table with: Metric Name | Current Value | Target | Unit | Owner. Flag any metrics without owners or targets. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Identifies measurement framework embedded in business documents for dashboard design.
tips: Copilot works best when metrics have explicit values; for qualitative targets, ask it to infer quantifiable proxies. Useful for OKR setting.

title: Competitive Positioning Summary
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. From these [NUMBER] sources about our competitors, summarize: what do they do well, where are gaps, how are we positioned against each? Highlight our differentiation opportunities. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Synthesizes competitive intelligence into strategy-ready briefs.
tips: Provide recent market research or news articles; Copilot will synthesize across sources. Ask for specific dimensions (pricing, features, service) for clearer comparison.

title: Quarterly Business Review Summary
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. Summarize this quarterly business review for [DIVISION]: Key metrics vs. targets (table format), top 3 wins, top 3 challenges, strategic initiatives status (On Track/At Risk/Off Track), and recommended priorities for next quarter. Keep to 2 pages max. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Condenses lengthy QBR materials into an executive-ready summary highlighting what matters most.
tips: Provide the raw QBR data or deck. Copilot will extract the signal from the noise. Focus on variance from plan rather than restating the plan.

title: Contract Key Terms Summary
prompt_text: Act as a senior business analyst with expertise in executive communications. Review this [CONTRACT_TYPE] and extract: 1) Key Terms (table: Clause | Summary | Risk Level), 2) Financial Obligations (amounts and dates and conditions), 3) Termination Provisions, 4) Liability and Indemnification, 5) Renewal Terms, 6) Red Flags or unusual clauses. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Extracts critical contract terms so decision-makers can review without reading the full document.
tips: Upload the contract or paste key sections. Copilot will flag non-standard clauses. Always have legal review before signing — this is a summary not legal advice.

title: Organizational Chart Generator
prompt_text: Act as an organizational design specialist with expertise in hierarchy visualization. Create an organizational chart from this data: [ORG_DATA: employee list with reporting relationships]. Produce: (1) Hierarchy Structure — formatted as an indented tree showing reporting relationships (use: └── for direct reports, with indentation levels matching org depth), (2) Summary Statistics — total headcount, number of levels, average span of control per level, (3) Structural Analysis — table: Manager | Level | Direct Reports | Total Org Size | Span Assessment (Under/Appropriate/Over), (4) Anomalies — positions reporting to unexpected levels, skip-level relationships, single-person departments, managers with only 1 direct report, (5) Visualization Recommendations — suggest how to render this as a visual chart (horizontal vs. vertical, grouping by function vs. location, where to collapse detail). Think through how to handle: dotted-line relationships, vacant positions, interim/acting assignments, and shared services. Verify that every employee appears exactly once and that no reporting loops exist.
description: Generates organizational charts from data with structural analysis, anomaly detection, and visualization recommendations.
tips: Provide employee data with at minimum: name, title, reports-to. Add "Also include [COST_CENTER/LOCATION/DEPARTMENT]" for richer grouping options. For large orgs (500+), add "Focus on [DIVISION] and show other areas collapsed."

title: Process Flow Diagram Creator
prompt_text: Act as a business process analyst with expertise in workflow visualization and process mapping. Create a process flow diagram for [PROCESS_NAME] based on this description: [PROCESS_DESCRIPTION]. Produce: (1) Process Steps — numbered sequence of steps with: Step # | Action | Actor (role) | System/Tool | Input | Output | Decision Point (Y/N), (2) Swimlane Layout — organize steps by actor/role showing handoffs clearly, (3) Decision Points — for each branch: condition, Yes path, No path, and any loops, (4) Exception Paths — what happens when the normal flow is disrupted (errors, rejections, escalations), (5) Timing — estimated duration per step and total end-to-end time, (6) Improvement Opportunities — where you see bottlenecks, redundant steps, or automation potential. Render the flow using text-based notation: [START] → Step 1 → <Decision?> → [Yes] Step 2a / [No] Step 2b → [END]. Think through edge cases: what triggers the process, what happens if it stalls at any step, and how does it end (successfully and unsuccessfully). Verify that every decision point has all branches defined and that the process has a clear start and end.
description: Creates process flow diagrams with swimlanes, decision points, exception paths, and improvement opportunities.
tips: Describe the process in detail, including who does what and in which system. Add "The biggest pain point is [STEP/HANDOFF]" to focus improvement recommendations. For complex processes, start with the "happy path" then add exceptions.

title: Timeline and Milestone Visualizer
prompt_text: Act as a project visualization specialist with expertise in timeline and roadmap design. Create a visual timeline for [PROJECT_OR_INITIATIVE] from this data: [MILESTONES_AND_DATES]. Produce: (1) Timeline Layout — chronological display: Date | Milestone | Owner | Status (Complete ✓ / On Track → / At Risk ⚠ / Overdue ✗), (2) Phase Grouping — organize milestones into logical phases with start/end dates for each phase, (3) Dependencies — show which milestones depend on others completing first (use: A → B notation), (4) Critical Path — highlight the sequence of milestones that determines the overall timeline (delay here = project delay), (5) Upcoming Window — detail the next 30 days: what is due, who owns it, what needs to start, (6) Risk Highlights — milestones at risk of slipping based on current status and dependencies. Render as a text-based Gantt-style chart where feasible, using: ████ for completed, ░░░░ for remaining, ⚠ for at-risk. Verify that dates are logical (no milestone due before its dependency) and that every milestone has an owner.
description: Creates visual project timelines with milestone tracking, dependency mapping, and critical path identification.
tips: Provide milestones with dates, owners, and current status. Add "Our reporting audience is [EXECUTIVE/TEAM/STAKEHOLDER]" to calibrate detail level. For multi-workstream projects, add "Show workstreams as parallel lanes."

title: Case and Matter Lifecycle Tracker
prompt_text: Act as a case management specialist with expertise in lifecycle tracking and resolution management. Design a case/matter tracking framework for [CASE_TYPE: e.g., employee relations, compliance investigations, customer complaints, legal matters]: (1) Case Intake Template — fields to capture: case type, priority, involved parties, description, key dates, assigned handler, (2) Lifecycle Stages — define each stage with: entry criteria, required actions, exit criteria, and maximum duration, (3) Status Dashboard — table: Case ID | Type | Priority | Stage | Days Open | Handler | Next Action | Next Action Due, (4) Aging Report — cases exceeding expected duration by stage, with escalation recommendations, (5) Resolution Tracking — capture: outcome, root cause, time to resolution, satisfaction rating, (6) Pattern Analysis — across the portfolio: most common case types, average resolution time, repeat issues, systemic root causes. Think through confidentiality: which case details should be visible to whom? Verify that the framework handles reopened cases and that no case can exist without an assigned handler.
description: Designs case lifecycle management frameworks with intake, tracking, aging reports, and pattern analysis.
tips: Define your case types and priority levels upfront. Add "Cases involving [SENSITIVE_TOPIC] have restricted visibility" for privacy-sensitive processes. For legal matters, add mandatory hold/preservation checkpoints.

title: Dynamic Approver Identification
prompt_text: Act as a governance specialist with expertise in delegation of authority and approval workflows. Design the approver identification logic for [REQUEST_TYPE: e.g., purchase requisitions, job offers, policy exceptions, travel requests]. Build the approval matrix: (1) Standard Approval Rules — table: Request Type | Amount/Threshold | Required Approver(s) | Sequence (parallel or sequential), (2) Exception Rules — conditions that require additional approvers: cross-departmental, above budget, new vendor, regulatory implications, (3) Delegation Logic — when the designated approver is unavailable: backup approver identification, time-based auto-escalation, delegation of authority documentation, (4) Conflict of Interest Checks — when should the approver be disqualified (e.g., approving their own expense, approving for a direct report), (5) Multi-Signature Requirements — which requests need more than one approver and why. Format as a decision tree: IF [condition] THEN [approver] ELSE [fallback]. Verify that every request type resolves to at least one approver and that no approval can be self-authorized. Note any scenarios where the rules are ambiguous and require policy clarification.
description: Designs dynamic approver identification logic with delegation, conflict of interest checks, and exception handling.
tips: Provide your current delegation of authority matrix. Add "Our approval thresholds are [AMOUNTS]" and "Regional differences include [VARIATIONS]." For complex organizations, start with the 5 most common request types.

title: Delegation of Authority Mapper
prompt_text: Act as a corporate governance specialist with expertise in delegation of authority frameworks. Design or review a delegation of authority (DoA) matrix for [ORGANIZATION_OR_DEPARTMENT]: (1) Authority Matrix — table: Decision Type | Threshold Levels | Approver at Each Level | Conditions/Restrictions, (2) Threshold Tiers — define clear breakpoints (e.g., <$5K manager, $5-25K director, $25-100K VP, >$100K C-suite) with rationale for each threshold, (3) Special Authorities — decisions requiring specific expertise regardless of amount (legal, HR, safety, regulatory), (4) Interim/Acting Arrangements — how authority transfers during absences, vacancies, and reorganizations, (5) Audit Trail Requirements — what documentation must accompany each approval level, (6) Review Cadence — when should thresholds be reassessed (annually, after org changes, after audit findings). Verify that the matrix has no gaps (every decision type at every threshold has a clear approver) and no overlaps (no ambiguity about who approves). Flag any thresholds that seem misaligned with industry benchmarks or organizational risk tolerance.
description: Maps delegation of authority with threshold tiers, special authorities, interim arrangements, and audit requirements.
tips: Provide your current DoA if reviewing, or industry benchmarks if creating new. Add "Our risk appetite is [CONSERVATIVE/MODERATE/AGGRESSIVE]" to calibrate thresholds. For multinational organizations, note jurisdictional requirements for local authority.

title: Workplace Experience Program
prompt_text: Act as a facilities management expert with expertise in operations and sustainability. Design a workplace experience program for [LOCATION]: 1) Current State Assessment (employee satisfaction and utilization), 2) Program Pillars (e.g. collaboration spaces/wellness/technology), 3) Initiative Roadmap (table: Initiative | Cost | Timeline | Expected Impact), 4) Success Metrics, 5) Feedback Loop mechanism. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Creates a holistic workplace improvement plan that drives employee satisfaction and space utilization.
tips: Survey employees first to understand what they actually want. Prioritize quick wins alongside longer-term investments. Measure before and after.

title: Space Planning Guidance
prompt_text: Act as a workplace strategy consultant with expertise in space optimization and hybrid work design. For this [OFFICE/FACILITY], we're planning [PROJECT_SCOPE: expansion, reconfiguration, contraction]. Current: [CURRENT_STATE]. Goal: [GOAL: cost reduction, collaboration, flexibility]. What's the optimal layout? What trade-offs?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Designs workplace layouts aligned with business and employee needs.
tips: Involve employees in planning; Copilot will suggest layouts based on space needs. Consider future flexibility.

Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.

tips: Use real sensor data or proxy metrics; Copilot will interpret. Consider work patterns (hybrid, in-office) in analysis.

description: Aligns workplace design with modern work models and flexibility needs.
tips: Survey employees on work preferences; Copilot will guide design. Model cost/headcount implications.

Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.

Calculate: occupancy rate (%), utilization trend, and cost per employee. Is space being used effectively?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Tracks real estate utilization for space and cost optimization.
tips: Use badge data or survey; Copilot will calculate efficiency. Compare across facilities and time.

title: Social Media Content Creator
prompt_text: Act as a social media content strategist for a financial services brand. Create a complete social media post package for [PLATFORM: LinkedIn/Twitter/Instagram] about [TOPIC/CAMPAIGN]. Deliver: (1) post copy — professional yet engaging tone appropriate for regulated financial services (avoid promissory language, performance guarantees, or unsubstantiated claims), (2) a matching visual concept description I can generate as an image, (3) 3-5 relevant hashtags, (4) a suggested posting time based on [AUDIENCE: e.g., industry professionals, retail clients, employees]. Flag any content that may require compliance review before publishing.
description: Creates compliant social media content packages (copy + visual concept + hashtags) tailored for financial services.
tips: All external-facing social media content at regulated institutions typically requires compliance review. Use this as a starting draft, not final copy. Copilot Designer can generate the matching visual.

title: Escalation and Handoff Pattern
prompt_text: Act as a conversational AI designer with expertise in enterprise bot development. Design escalation workflow for an agent: when should requests go to humans (complex, out-of-scope)? How to ensure smooth handoff? What context to pass? How to track escalations?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures seamless human handoff when agent can't help.
tips: Build escalation criteria and context passing; Copilot Studio will route. Track escalation quality for improvements.

title: Exception Handling Bot
prompt_text: Act as a governance and compliance specialist with expertise in policy management. Build an exception handling bot: 1) Exception request intake (policy/reason/duration), 2) Policy lookup and display of current rule, 3) Risk assessment questions, 4) Auto-approve for low-risk pre-defined exceptions, 5) Route high-risk exceptions to appropriate approver, 6) Expiry tracking and renewal reminders. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Standardizes exception handling while maintaining governance controls and audit trail.
tips: Define which exceptions can be auto-approved. Set maximum duration for all exceptions. Build in automatic expiry — no permanent exceptions without review.

title: HR FAQ Bot
prompt_text: Act as a conversational AI designer with expertise in enterprise bot development. Design an HR FAQ bot in Copilot Studio that answers: policy questions, benefits info, leave requests, career development, and escalation to HR for complex issues. Knowledge base: [POLICIES]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.

tips: Build in knowledge base with HR policies and FAQs; Copilot Studio will handle routing. Include escalation for edge cases.

title: Guardrail and Safety Pattern
prompt_text: Act as a conversational AI designer with expertise in enterprise bot development. Design guardrails for [AGENT_TYPE]: what should the agent refuse (e.g., policy exceptions, confidential data)? How to detect out-of-scope requests? When to escalate to human?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.

Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Reduces ticket volume by resolving common issues automatically and routing complex ones correctly.
tips: Map your top 20 ticket types. Build troubleshooting trees for each. Connect to ServiceNow or Jira for ticket creation. Track deflection rate.

Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Provides instant access to organizational knowledge while identifying gaps that need documentation.
tips: Start with a focused knowledge domain. Measure answer quality through user feedback. Use gap detection to prioritize new content creation.

Design an agent that triages incoming emails: summarizes, categorizes by urgency/theme, identifies action items, and suggests responses. Works with email plugin. Reduces manual inbox work. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Accelerates marketing brief creation with consistency.

Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.

tips: Build intake flow with conditional logic; Copilot Studio will handle multi-turn conversation. Connect to procurement system.

title: Automated Report Generator Bot
prompt_text: Act as a business intelligence specialist with expertise in automated reporting. Build a reporting bot that: 1) Accepts report requests via natural language (what report/what period/what filters), 2) Queries data sources (SQL/SharePoint/API), 3) Generates formatted output (table/chart/summary), 4) Delivers via Teams or email, 5) Schedules recurring reports, 6) Handles ad-hoc drill-down questions. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
