tips: Start with 3-5 most requested reports. Build templates for each. Add new reports based on usage patterns. Always include data freshness timestamp.

title: Testing and Validation Pattern
prompt_text: Act as a conversational AI designer with expertise in enterprise bot development. Design a testing approach for a Copilot Studio agent: test cases (happy path, edge cases, escalation), evaluation criteria, and iteration workflow. How to validate before launch?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures agent quality before production.
tips: Build test cases covering main and edge scenarios; Copilot Studio will help. Get feedback from pilot users.

title: Cross-System Record Enrichment
prompt_text: Act as a data integration specialist with expertise in enterprise record enrichment. I need to enrich records from [PRIMARY_SYSTEM] with data from [SECONDARY_SYSTEM(S)]. Design the enrichment: (1) Join Strategy — identify the linking key(s) between systems (employee ID, email, cost center, etc.) and assess match reliability, (2) Enrichment Fields — table: Field to Add | Source System | Join Key | Match Rate | Fallback if No Match, (3) Derived Fields — calculate new fields from combined data: [SPECIFY: e.g., tenure = today minus hire date, risk score = function of X + Y + Z], (4) Orphan Handling — records that exist in the primary but not secondary system: flag, report, and recommend resolution, (5) Quality Validation — after enrichment, verify: no duplicate records created, no data loss from the primary source, enrichment coverage rate per field. Format as an enrichment specification document. Note any assumptions about data quality or system-of-record authority.
description: Designs cross-system record enrichment with join strategy, derived field calculation, and quality validation.
tips: Always establish which system is the "source of truth" for each field. Start with a small sample to validate join accuracy before running at scale. Add "Include manager hierarchy from [HR_SYSTEM]" for org-aware enrichment.

title: Organizational Hierarchy Resolution
prompt_text: Act as an HRIS data specialist with expertise in organizational hierarchy management. I need to resolve and validate organizational relationships in this dataset: (1) Manager Chain — for each employee, trace the reporting chain up to [LEVEL: e.g., C-suite]; flag broken chains (manager not found, circular references, self-reporting), (2) Cost Center Assignment — validate that every employee has a valid cost center; flag mismatches where employee and manager have different cost centers without a documented exception, (3) Approver Resolution — based on [APPROVAL_RULES], determine the correct approver for each record type; document the decision path, (4) Span of Control Analysis — calculate direct and indirect reports per manager; flag outliers (>15 directs or <2), (5) Data Quality Issues — table: Issue Type | Count | Examples | Impact | Recommended Fix. Think through hierarchy edge cases: acting roles, dual-reporting (matrix), vacant positions, recent org changes. Verify that every employee resolves to exactly one active manager and one valid cost center.
description: Resolves and validates organizational hierarchies, manager chains, cost centers, and approval authorities.
tips: Provide the organizational hierarchy extract from your HRIS. Add "Recent org changes include [CHANGES]" to account for transition periods. For matrix organizations, specify which reporting line takes precedence.

title: Framework Conformance Validator
prompt_text: Act as a quality assurance specialist with expertise in standards compliance and framework validation. Review this [DOCUMENT_OR_DELIVERABLE] against [FRAMEWORK/STANDARD: e.g., 5Ws (Who/What/When/Where/Why), SMART goals, RACI matrix, policy template standard, SOX controls, ISO requirements]. For each framework element: (1) Compliance Assessment — table: Framework Element | Required | Present in Document? | Quality (Complete/Partial/Missing) | Evidence, (2) Gap Analysis — elements that are missing or incomplete, with specific recommendations to address each gap, (3) Quality Rating — each element scored as: Exemplary (exceeds standard) / Compliant (meets standard) / Deficient (below standard) / Missing (not present), (4) Overall Conformance Score — percentage of framework elements rated Compliant or better, (5) Remediation Priority — gaps ranked by: business impact if not addressed, effort to fix, and deadline sensitivity. Think through whether partial compliance is acceptable or if each element is pass/fail. Verify your assessment by cross-referencing the framework definition — do not assess against memory of the framework; use the actual specification.
description: Validates documents and deliverables against specified frameworks and standards with gap analysis and remediation priorities.
tips: Provide or reference the specific framework/standard being validated against. Add "Our minimum acceptable conformance level is [X]%" for pass/fail determination. For regulatory frameworks, add "Non-compliance with [ELEMENTS] carries legal risk."

title: Learning Program Design
prompt_text: Act as a learning and development strategist with expertise in capability building. Design a learning program for [TOPIC]. Audience: [AUDIENCE]. Format: [FORMAT: online, classroom, blended]. Duration: [DURATION]. Structure: modules, assessments, and reinforcement plan. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Blueprints learning experiences aligned with content and learner needs.
tips: Start with learning objectives; design backwards. Include pre-work, main content, assessment, and reinforcement. Copilot will suggest sequencing.

title: Talent Review Brief
prompt_text: Act as a senior talent acquisition strategist with expertise in inclusive hiring. Prepare a quarterly talent review brief: high-potentials, flight risks, succession candidates, key development needs. Include: assessment summary, calibrated ratings, and action plan. Audience: EXEC_TEAM. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Synthesizes talent assessment into executive briefing for decisions.
tips: Use performance, feedback, and development data; maintain confidentiality. Present actionable recommendations.

title: Organizational Design & Restructuring
prompt_text: Act as a workforce planning specialist with expertise in organizational design. Design org structure for [TEAM_OR_DEPARTMENT]: 1) Current State (org chart and headcount), 2) Proposed Structure (chart and FTE impact), 3) Role Definitions (table: Title | Responsibilities | Seniority | Reporting Line), 4) Transition Plan with timeline, 5) Cost Impact Analysis. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Supports organizational redesign with clear change impact and cost and headcount shift tracking.
tips: Build current org chart in a table format. Copilot will draft proposed structure options and cost calculations. Include compensation ranges for accuracy.

title: Interview Scorecard System
prompt_text: Act as a senior talent acquisition strategist with expertise in inclusive hiring. Design an interview scorecard for [ROLE]: 1) Core Competencies to assess (table: Competency | Definition | Behavioral Indicators), 2) Scoring Rubric (1-5 scale with anchors), 3) Interview Questions mapped to competencies, 4) Red Flags to watch for, 5) Overall Assessment Template. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Creates consistent and defensible interview evaluation across all interviewers for a role.
tips: Align competencies with job description. Train interviewers on the rubric before use. Score independently before debrief to avoid groupthink.

title: Living Knowledge Base Updater
prompt_text: Act as a knowledge base curator with expertise in content lifecycle management. I have new information: [NEW_CONTENT/UPDATES] that needs to be integrated into our existing knowledge base on [TOPIC]. Perform the update: (1) Impact Assessment — what existing content is affected by this new information: table: Existing Section | Change Needed | Type (Update/Replace/Archive/No Change), (2) Content Updates — for each affected section, show: current text → proposed update, with change rationale, (3) New Additions — content that should be added as new sections, with recommended placement in the knowledge base structure, (4) Archival Candidates — existing content that is now outdated or superseded; recommend archive vs. delete, (5) Cross-Reference Updates — links, references, or dependencies that need updating because of these changes, (6) Version Notes — summary of changes made for the changelog. Preserve the existing tone, format, and structure of the knowledge base. Think through downstream impacts: what other documents reference this knowledge base? Verify that no existing accurate information was accidentally removed or contradicted.
description: Integrates new information into existing knowledge bases with impact assessment, version tracking, and cross-reference updates.
tips: Provide the current knowledge base content and the new information separately. Add "Our knowledge base follows [STRUCTURE/TEMPLATE]" for consistent updates. For regulated content, add "Changes require review by [APPROVER] before publishing."

title: Precedent and Case Research
prompt_text: Act as a research analyst with expertise in precedent analysis and institutional knowledge. I need to find relevant precedents for this situation: [SITUATION_DESCRIPTION]. Search available documents, case files, and prior decisions to identify: (1) Directly Relevant Precedents — cases or decisions with similar facts or issues: table: Case/Decision | Date | Key Facts | Outcome | Decision Maker, (2) Analogous Situations — cases that aren't identical but share relevant characteristics, with explanation of the analogy, (3) Distinguishing Factors — what makes this situation different from prior cases (could lead to a different outcome), (4) Pattern Analysis — do prior decisions show a consistent approach, or is there inconsistency, (5) Recommendation — based on precedent, what is the most likely/appropriate outcome, with confidence level. Think through which prior decisions are most authoritative and recent. Note any precedents that were later overturned or superseded. Verify that you are comparing like-for-like situations and not drawing false analogies.
description: Researches relevant precedents and prior decisions to inform current situation analysis.
tips: Provide access to case files or decision logs. Add "The most relevant prior cases are in [SYSTEM/FOLDER]" to focus the search. For legal precedent, always note jurisdiction and whether the precedent is binding or persuasive.

title: Outlier and Anomaly Detection
prompt_text: Act as a statistical analyst with expertise in anomaly detection and exception reporting. Analyze this [DATA_TYPE] for outliers and anomalies: (1) Statistical Outliers — identify values more than [X: default 2] standard deviations from the mean for each numeric field; table: Field | Value | Mean | StdDev | Z-Score | Record ID, (2) Behavioral Anomalies — patterns that deviate from historical norms: sudden changes, unusual combinations of values, activity outside normal hours/patterns, (3) Business Rule Violations — values that violate known constraints: [SPECIFY_RULES: e.g., salary cannot exceed band maximum, approval amounts have delegation limits], (4) Trend Breaks — metrics that suddenly changed direction or magnitude compared to prior [PERIOD], (5) Risk Assessment — for each anomaly: Likely Cause (data error / legitimate exception / potential fraud / process failure) and Recommended Investigation Action. Rank anomalies by severity: Critical (requires immediate investigation) / Moderate (review within 1 week) / Low (monitor). Explain your statistical methodology and note where sample sizes are too small for reliable detection.
description: Detects statistical outliers, behavioral anomalies, and business rule violations with risk-ranked investigation recommendations.
tips: Provide historical baseline data for better anomaly detection. Specify known business rules and constraints. Add "False positive tolerance: [LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH]" to calibrate sensitivity. For financial data, focus on round-number amounts and just-below-threshold values.

title: Alert and Escalation Rule Builder
prompt_text: Act as a process automation architect with expertise in alert and escalation design. Design an alerting framework for [PROCESS_OR_SYSTEM]: (1) Alert Conditions — table: Trigger Event | Threshold | Severity (P1-P4) | Example Scenario, (2) Notification Matrix — who gets notified at each severity: table: Severity | Primary Recipient | Escalation To (if no response in X hours) | Channel (email/Teams/phone), (3) Escalation Ladder — define time-based escalation: if P1 not acknowledged in 15 min → escalate to [ROLE]; if still unresolved in 1 hour → escalate to [ROLE], (4) Alert Suppression Rules — prevent alert fatigue: batch similar alerts, suppress during maintenance windows, deduplicate recurring alerts, (5) Response Playbook — for each alert type: first 3 steps the responder should take, (6) Measurement — track: alert volume, response time, false positive rate, mean time to resolution. Think through edge cases: what if the primary recipient is unavailable? What if multiple alerts fire simultaneously? Verify that every alert has a clear owner and that escalation paths don't create loops.
description: Designs comprehensive alerting and escalation frameworks with notification routing, suppression logic, and response playbooks.
tips: Start with your highest-impact scenarios. Add "Our current pain points with alerts are [ISSUES]" to focus on real problems. For large organizations, design alerts by role/team rather than individual to handle turnover.

title: Crisis Communication Plan
prompt_text: Act as a brand strategist and communications expert. Prepare a crisis communication plan: anticipated crises for [COMPANY/INDUSTRY], response templates, approval chains, and key messages. How will we respond quickly while protecting reputation?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Prepares organization for reputation-threatening situations.
tips: Identify credible crisis scenarios; Copilot will draft response templates. Align with legal/compliance on messaging.

title: Stakeholder Communication"

Channels/timing: [PLAN]. How to manage expectations and concerns?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Coordinates stakeholder communication to ensure alignment and understanding.
tips: Tailor message for each stakeholder type; Copilot will help. Anticipate concerns and address proactively.

title: Employee Advocacy Program
prompt_text: Act as a brand strategist and communications expert. Design an employee advocacy program: goal (amplify reach? brand awareness?), content, incentives, and ease of participation. How will employees share and what should they say?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Mobilizes employees as authentic brand ambassadors for greater reach.
tips: Make it easy to participate; provide content and talking points. Track participation and impact; celebrate advocates.

title: Brand Voice Guidelines
prompt_text: Act as a brand governance specialist with expertise in voice consistency and standards. Develop brand voice guidelines for [BRAND]: 1) Brand Personality (3-5 traits with definitions), 2) Voice Characteristics (table: We Are | We Are Not), 3) Tone Spectrum by channel (social vs. formal vs. support), 4) Writing Do's and Don'ts with examples, 5) Sample copy for key touchpoints. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Codifies brand voice so all content creators produce consistent messaging across channels.
tips: Include real before/after examples. Test guidelines with a team member who was not involved in creating them. Review quarterly as brand evolves.

title: Content Refinement
prompt_text: Act as a senior marketing strategist with expertise in campaign performance and ROI optimization. Review this marketing content [PIECE]: [CONTENT]. Improve: clarity, persuasiveness, brand voice, call-to-action, and fit with audience. Preserve message intent; tighten by [%]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Enhances content for greater impact and engagement.
tips: Describe target audience and conversion goal; Copilot will optimize for impact. A/B test versions if possible.

title: A/B Testing Strategy
prompt_text: Act as a performance marketing strategist with expertise in data-driven campaign optimization. Design an A/B testing plan for [CAMPAIGN_OR_PAGE]: 1) Hypothesis (what we believe and why), 2) Test Variables (what changes between A and B), 3) Success Metrics (primary and secondary), 4) Sample Size calculation, 5) Test Duration, 6) Statistical Significance threshold, 7) Decision Framework (what we do with results). Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output.
description: Creates rigorous testing plans that generate actionable insights rather than inconclusive data.
tips: Test one variable at a time. Ensure sample size is large enough for significance. Define success criteria before running the test — do not move goalposts.

title: Brand Strategy Brief
prompt_text: Act as a brand strategist and communications expert. Develop a brand strategy brief: current positioning, target audience, desired perception, key messages, and brand experience. How should we evolve to stay relevant? What's our brand promise?. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Comprehensive brand strategy guiding all marketing and communications.
tips: Use customer research, competitive analysis, and brand assessment; Copilot will synthesize. Validate with leadership and customers.

title: Annual Vendor Review
prompt_text: Act as a strategic sourcing specialist with expertise in vendor performance management. Prepare an annual vendor review: performance summary, cost trend, risk assessment, and recommendations (continue, consolidate, replace, or expand). Audience: procurement leadership. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Executive briefing on vendor portfolio health and strategic actions.
tips: Use vendor scorecard, spend data, and market intelligence; Copilot will synthesize. Include action items.

title: Policy Rule Interpreter
prompt_text: Act as a policy analyst with expertise in [POLICY_DOMAIN: e.g., immigration, compensation, employee relations, learning, payroll, risk]. I need to interpret and apply the following policy: [POLICY_NAME or POLICY_TEXT]. For this specific situation: [SITUATION_DESCRIPTION]. Walk through the analysis: (1) Applicable Rules — which specific policy provisions apply to this situation, with section references, (2) Rule Application — step-by-step application of each rule to the facts, showing your reasoning at each decision point, (3) Decision — what the policy requires, permits, or prohibits, (4) Exceptions — are there any exceptions, exemptions, or discretionary provisions that could apply, (5) Rationale — plain-language explanation of WHY the policy leads to this outcome (suitable for communicating to the affected individual), (6) Caveats — what additional information would change the analysis, and when should this be escalated to Legal/HR/Compliance. Do not provide legal advice — frame as policy interpretation. Flag any ambiguity in the policy text that requires authoritative clarification.
description: Interprets business policies for specific situations with step-by-step rule application and plain-language rationale.
tips: Provide the actual policy text or section numbers. Be specific about the situation facts. Add "This person is in [JURISDICTION]" for location-specific rules. Always have the output reviewed by the policy owner before acting on it.

title: Jurisdictional Logic Analyzer
prompt_text: Act as a regulatory compliance analyst with expertise in multi-jurisdictional employment and business law. I need to understand how [POLICY_OR_REGULATION] applies differently across jurisdictions: [JURISDICTIONS: e.g., US Federal, Ontario, Quebec, BC, Alberta]. For each jurisdiction: (1) Applicable Law — the governing statute or regulation, (2) Key Requirements — what the law requires, with specific thresholds and deadlines, (3) Differences from Federal/National Standard — what is more or less restrictive, (4) Compliance Actions — what we specifically need to do in this jurisdiction. Format as a comparison matrix: Requirement | Federal | Jurisdiction A | Jurisdiction B | Jurisdiction C | Most Restrictive. Then provide: (5) Practical Guidance — recommended approach that satisfies ALL jurisdictions simultaneously where possible, (6) Jurisdiction-Specific Exceptions — where a one-size-fits-all approach won't work. Verify that your analysis reflects the most current version of each regulation. Flag any recent or pending changes that could affect compliance.
description: Compares regulatory requirements across jurisdictions with a compliance matrix and practical guidance.
tips: Specify the exact jurisdictions relevant to your operations. Add "We have employees in [LOCATIONS]" for targeted analysis. For US-Canada comparisons, note that Canadian provincial law often differs significantly from federal. Always validate with local legal counsel.

title: Agreement and Contract Template Population
prompt_text: Act as a contract administration specialist with expertise in agreement preparation. Using our [TEMPLATE_TYPE: e.g., NDA, vendor agreement, consulting agreement, licensing agreement] template, populate the variable fields with: [DEAL_DETAILS]. (1) Field Population — complete all bracketed/highlighted fields: party names, dates, amounts, scope, term, notice addresses, (2) Clause Selection — where the template offers alternative clauses (e.g., indemnification options, liability caps), recommend the appropriate option based on [RISK_PROFILE] and explain your reasoning, (3) Schedule/Exhibit Completion — populate any attached schedules with: scope of work, pricing tables, SLAs, deliverables, (4) Consistency Check — verify that: party names are consistent throughout, dates are logical (start before end), cross-references are correct, defined terms match their usage, (5) Missing Information — flag fields that could not be populated and specify what information is needed. Do not modify standard legal language — only populate variable fields. Verify mathematical accuracy of all financial terms. Flag any unusual deal terms that deviate from our standard template.
description: Populates contract templates with deal-specific information, clause selection, and consistency validation.
tips: Provide the template and all deal details. Add "Our standard terms for [DEAL_TYPE] include [KEY_TERMS]" to guide clause selection. Always have Legal review the completed agreement before execution.

title: Executive Leader Custom Instructions
prompt_text: Act as a Copilot configuration specialist who designs personalized AI workflows. Set up Copilot for an executive leader who needs: fast briefings on strategic topics, board-ready content, competitive intelligence synthesis, and quick decisions on major investments. Include: tone preferences, output formats, key contexts, and gotchas to avoid. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Personalizes Copilot for executive workflows and communication style.
tips: Build the template; user refines based on first few uses. Covers reporting style, preferred brevity, risk tolerance, and decision-support needs.

title: Data Privacy Guardian
prompt_text: Act as a governance and compliance specialist with expertise in policy management. Custom Instruction: When I share documents or data, always: 1) Flag any PII or sensitive data before processing, 2) Suggest redaction where appropriate, 3) Never include customer names or account numbers in summaries, 4) Remind me of data classification if content appears confidential, 5) Default to internal-only classification unless told otherwise. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Adds a data privacy layer to all Copilot interactions to prevent accidental data exposure.
tips: Set this as a persistent custom instruction. Review monthly to align with updated privacy policies. Combine with DLP policies for comprehensive protection.

title: Executive Communication Style
prompt_text: Act as a senior executive communications writer who specializes in leadership-ready content. Custom Instruction: Format all my outputs for executive audiences: 1) Lead with the conclusion or recommendation, 2) Maximum 1 page unless I specify longer, 3) Use tables over paragraphs for data, 4) Include a clear Ask or Decision Needed section, 5) Bold key numbers and dates, 6) End with Next Steps and owners. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Forces all Copilot output into concise executive-ready format automatically.
tips: Combine with department-specific instructions for maximum impact. Override with specific formatting when needed for different audiences.

title: Project Management Enforcer
prompt_text: Act as a certified project manager with expertise in stakeholder communication. Custom Instruction: For all project-related requests: 1) Always include Owner/DRI for each action item, 2) Include specific dates not vague timelines, 3) Flag dependencies and risks, 4) Use RAG status (Red/Amber/Green) for status updates, 5) Ask me for missing information rather than assuming, 6) Track against previously stated milestones. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Adds project management rigor to all Copilot interactions automatically.
tips: Set at the beginning of a project. Update milestones as they change. Works best when combined with a project charter or scope document.

title: Brand Voice Consistency
prompt_text: Act as a brand governance specialist with expertise in voice consistency and standards. Custom Instruction: All content I create should follow our brand voice: 1) Tone: [SPECIFY - e.g. professional yet approachable], 2) Avoid: [SPECIFY - e.g. jargon/passive voice/exclamation marks], 3) Always: [SPECIFY - e.g. use active voice/include call to action], 4) Formatting: [SPECIFY - e.g. short paragraphs/bullet points for features], 5) Terminology: Use [PREFERRED_TERMS] instead of [AVOIDED_TERMS]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Ensures all Copilot-generated content adheres to brand guidelines without manual review.
tips: Fill in your specific brand voice attributes. Share with the marketing team for consistency. Review quarterly as brand evolves.

title: Financial Analysis Standards
prompt_text: Act as a financial analyst with expertise in modeling and variance analysis. Custom Instruction: For all financial analysis: 1) Show numbers in thousands with one decimal (e.g. $1.2K), 2) Always include YoY and QoQ comparisons, 3) Use RAG indicators for variance (Green: within 5%, Yellow: 5-10%, Red: >10%), 4) Include source data reference, 5) Flag assumptions explicitly, 6) Default currency: [SPECIFY]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Standardizes financial output format for consistency across all Copilot-generated analysis.
tips: Align thresholds with your company's materiality standards. Share with finance team for adoption. Update currency and formatting for regional teams.

title: Meeting Productivity Protocol
prompt_text: Act as a productivity and operations specialist. Custom Instruction: For all meeting-related tasks: 1) Agenda items must have time allocations, 2) Notes must capture decisions separately from discussion, 3) Action items must have owner and deadline, 4) Flag agenda items that could be an email instead, 5) Follow-up summaries sent within 2 hours, 6) Track carryover items from previous meetings. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Brings structure and accountability to meeting workflows through consistent Copilot behavior.
tips: Apply to Teams meeting recaps. Works best when meeting organizers adopt the same standard. Review action items at the start of the next meeting.

title: Quality Assurance Checker
prompt_text: Act as a quality assurance specialist with expertise in output validation. Custom Instruction: Before finalizing any output, automatically check: 1) Spelling and grammar, 2) Consistency of formatting (headers/bullets/numbering), 3) Accuracy of any calculations or numbers, 4) Completeness (are all requested sections present), 5) Tone appropriateness for the stated audience, 6) Flag any claims that need verification. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Adds an automatic quality check layer to all Copilot outputs before delivery.
tips: Enable as a persistent instruction. Copilot will self-review before presenting output. Reduces review cycles and catches common errors.

title: Regulatory Compliance Filter
prompt_text: Act as a regulatory compliance specialist with expertise in risk assessment. Custom Instruction: Apply these compliance checks to all content: 1) Flag language that could be construed as a guarantee or promise, 2) Ensure disclaimers are present for [SPECIFY - e.g. financial/medical/legal content], 3) Check for inclusive language, 4) Flag any references to competitors by name, 5) Ensure all claims have supporting evidence, 6) Default to conservative/cautious framing for external communications. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Automatically screens Copilot output for common compliance and regulatory risks.
tips: Customize for your industry regulations. Have compliance team review and approve the instruction set. Update when regulations change.

title: Risk Scoring Framework
prompt_text: Act as a senior data analyst with expertise in business intelligence. Create a risk score for [ENTITY_TYPE]: [ENTITIES]. Consider: [RISK_FACTORS]. For each entity, rate: likelihood (1-5), impact (1-5), and derive risk score (likelihood × impact). Rank and prioritize. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Operationalizes risk assessment for portfolio management and compliance.
tips: Define what each factor means (e.g., late payment = 3/5); Copilot will score consistently. Use this with governance reviews.

title: Financial Model Builder
prompt_text: Act as a data management specialist with expertise in data quality and governance. Build a financial model in this workbook for [SCENARIO]: Create sheets for 1) Assumptions (input cells highlighted in blue), 2) Revenue Model (by product line and by month), 3) Cost Structure (fixed vs. variable), 4) P&L Summary (with formulas linking to assumptions), 5) Sensitivity Analysis (what-if on key variables). Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Creates a linked financial model where changing assumptions automatically flows through to outputs.
tips: Use named ranges for key assumptions. Highlight input cells in blue and output cells in green. Include data validation dropdowns where applicable.

title: Workforce Planning Model
prompt_text: Act as a workforce planning specialist with expertise in organizational design. Build a workforce planning model: 1) Current Headcount by department and role and level, 2) Attrition Assumptions (historical turnover rate by segment), 3) Growth Plan (new positions by quarter), 4) Cost Model (fully loaded cost per FTE by level), 5) Gap Analysis (required vs. projected headcount), 6) Hiring Timeline. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Models workforce supply and demand with cost implications for budget planning.
tips: Use historical attrition data for accuracy. Include ramp time for new hires. Factor in internal mobility and promotions not just external hiring.

title: Customer Churn Analysis
prompt_text: Act as a senior analytics specialist with expertise in data-driven decision making. Analyze customer churn in [DATASET]: 1) Overall churn rate and trend, 2) Churn by segment (table: Segment | Churn Rate | Revenue Impact | Top Reason), 3) Early warning signals (behavioral patterns before churn), 4) Survival Analysis (time-to-churn distribution), 5) Retention Intervention Recommendations ranked by impact. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Identifies churn patterns and early warning indicators to enable proactive retention strategies.
tips: Include customer activity data (logins/usage/support tickets) alongside account data. Look for behavioral changes 30-60-90 days before churn.

title: Revenue Attribution Analysis
prompt_text: Act as a senior analytics specialist with expertise in data-driven decision making. Analyze revenue drivers in [DATASET]: 1) Revenue Decomposition (by product/channel/region/customer segment), 2) Growth Contribution Analysis (what drove YoY change), 3) Price vs. Volume vs. Mix Effects, 4) Cohort Revenue Analysis, 5) Revenue Concentration Risk (top 10 customers as % of total), 6) Forecast implications. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Decomposes revenue into component drivers to identify what is actually driving growth or decline.
tips: Include transaction-level data if possible. Copilot will aggregate and decompose. Watch for concentration risk — if top 10 customers are more than 30% of revenue flag it.

title: Negotiation Preparation Brief

Help me prepare: 1) Their likely priorities and constraints, 2) Our BATNA (best alternative), 3) Trading strategies (what we offer vs. what we need), 4) Recommended opening position, 5) Walk-away point, 6) Top 5 objections they might raise with counter-arguments. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Structured negotiation prep covering strategy and tactics and contingency scenarios.

title: Board Meeting Prep Package

title: Crisis Communication Playbook

title: Operational Playbook Section

title: Innovation Proposal Framework

title: Scenario Planning & Modeling

Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.

tips: Set up a simple dependency map upfront. Use notebook tables to organize outcomes. Include leading and lagging indicators for each scenario.

title: Financial Analysis & Forecasting
prompt_text: Act as a financial analyst with expertise in modeling and variance analysis. Analyze financial performance for [ENTITY_OR_PERIOD]: 1) Key Metrics (Revenue, EBITDA, FCF, Key Ratios), 2) Trend Analysis over [PERIODS], 3) Variance Analysis (Actual vs. Budget), 4) Forecast for next [PERIOD] with assumptions, 5) Risks and Opportunities. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.

title: Multi-Step Task Router
prompt_text: Act as a workflow automation designer with expertise in business process orchestration. Design a task routing workflow for [PROCESS_NAME]: (1) Intake Classification — how to categorize incoming items by type, urgency, and complexity; decision tree with branching criteria, (2) Routing Rules — table: Item Type | Routing Criteria | Assigned To (role, not person) | SLA | Fallback if Unassigned, (3) Handoff Protocol — when a task moves between stages or teams: what information transfers, what the next handler needs to know, confirmation of receipt, (4) Exception Handling — what happens when: item doesn't match any category, assignee is unavailable, SLA is breached, item needs to loop back to a previous step, (5) Status Tracking — define states (New → In Progress → Pending Review → Complete → Closed) with allowed transitions, (6) Automation Opportunities — which routing decisions can be fully automated vs. require human judgment. Think through volume: will this routing scale if volume doubles? Verify that every possible item type has a routing path and that no items can get "stuck" in a state with no exit.
description: Designs multi-step task routing workflows with classification rules, exception handling, and automation opportunities.
tips: Map your current manual routing process first. Add "Our biggest routing pain point is [ISSUE]" to focus the design. For high-volume processes, prioritize automation of the most common routing decisions.

title: Operating Expense Forecast
prompt_text: Act as a facilities management expert with expertise in operations and sustainability. Create an OpEx forecast model for [PORTFOLIO]: 1) Expense Categories (utilities/maintenance/security/cleaning/insurance), 2) Historical Spend (3 years), 3) Inflation Assumptions by category, 4) Forecast (monthly for 12 months), 5) Variance tracking vs. prior year, 6) Scenario toggle (base/optimistic/pessimistic). Think through each element systematically before producing output. Before finalizing, verify completeness, accuracy, and actionability of your output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Models CRE operating costs with inflation assumptions and scenario analysis for budget planning.
tips: Use actual invoices for historical accuracy. Utility costs are seasonal — model monthly not annual averages. Include contracted rate escalations.

title: Spreadsheet Data Normalization
prompt_text: Act as a data quality engineer with expertise in data normalization and ETL processes. I have a spreadsheet with data from [SOURCE_SYSTEM(S)]. Clean and normalize this data: (1) Field Standardization — normalize date formats to YYYY-MM-DD, currency to 2 decimals with consistent symbols, phone numbers to E.164, names to Title Case, (2) Schema Alignment — map columns to our standard schema: [TARGET_SCHEMA], identify unmapped fields, (3) Deduplication — identify potential duplicate records using fuzzy matching on [KEY_FIELDS], flag confidence level (Definite/Probable/Possible), (4) Validation Rules — check: required fields populated, values within expected ranges, referential integrity against [REFERENCE_LIST], (5) Data Quality Scorecard — table: Field | Completeness % | Validity % | Issues Found | Recommended Fix. Think through each column systematically. Flag records that require human review rather than auto-correcting ambiguous data.
description: Normalizes and cleans spreadsheet data with schema alignment, deduplication, and quality scoring.
tips: Specify the target schema and key fields for best results. Add "Our standard field names are [LIST]" to help with mapping. For large datasets, process in batches and request a summary of changes made.

Flag risks, unfavorable terms, and negotiation opportunities. Recommend changes for [YOUR_PRIORITY]. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Identifies contract risks and opportunities for negotiation.
tips: Use company standard templates; Copilot will highlight deviations. Involve legal for complex contracts.

title: Structured Layout from Unstructured Text
prompt_text: Act as a data structuring specialist with expertise in extracting organized information from unstructured text. I have unstructured text that needs to be converted into a structured format: [TEXT]. Transform this into: (1) Identify the Structure — what entities, attributes, and relationships exist in this text? Propose a schema: table columns and their data types, (2) Extract Data — populate the proposed structure with data from the text: table format with clean, normalized values, (3) Handle Ambiguity — where the text is unclear, show your interpretation and flag for human review, (4) Missing Data — identify fields that would logically exist but are not mentioned in the text (mark as "Not Specified"), (5) Validation — cross-check extracted values against each other for internal consistency (e.g., percentages that should sum to 100%, dates that should be chronological), (6) Output Format Recommendation — suggest the best format for this structured data (table, hierarchy, timeline, matrix) based on its nature. Think through whether the text contains one entity type or multiple that should be in separate tables. Verify that no information from the source text was lost in the structuring process.
description: Converts unstructured text into clean, structured tables and formats with validation and completeness checks.
tips: Provide the raw text and describe the intended use. Add "I need this in [TABLE/JSON/CSV/HIERARCHY] format" for specific output. For long documents, add "Focus on extracting [ENTITY_TYPE]" to narrow scope.

title: HR Onboarding Assistant Bot
prompt_text: Act as an HR operations specialist with expertise in employee lifecycle management. Build a Copilot Studio bot that guides new hires through onboarding: 1) Welcome message with company overview, 2) Document checklist (tax forms/benefits enrollment/IT setup), 3) FAQ responses for common Day 1 questions, 4) Meeting scheduler for buddy/manager introductions, 5) Escalation to HR generalist for complex questions. Think through each element systematically before producing output. Note any assumptions made and flag areas where additional information would improve the analysis.
description: Automates the repetitive parts of onboarding while maintaining a personal touch for new employees.
tips: Use Adaptive Cards for document checklists. Connect to SharePoint for policy documents. Include a feedback loop to improve answers over time.

title: Cross-Source Data Alignment
prompt_text: Act as a data integration architect with expertise in schema reconciliation. I have data from [SOURCE_A] and [SOURCE_B] that need to be aligned into a unified view. Walk through this systematically: (1) Schema Comparison — table: Source A Field | Source B Field | Match Type (Exact/Similar/Unmatched) | Transformation Needed, (2) Key Resolution — identify the join key(s) and flag records that exist in one source but not the other (orphan analysis), (3) Conflict Resolution — where both sources have values for the same field but disagree, document the conflict and recommend which source to trust based on [PRIORITY_RULES: e.g., Workday is authoritative for employee data], (4) Gap Analysis — fields present in one source but missing in the other, with business impact, (5) Unified Schema — proposed merged schema with source attribution. Format conflicts as: Record ID | Field | Source A Value | Source B Value | Recommended Value | Rationale. Before finalizing, verify that no records were lost in the alignment and that all critical fields have a resolution path.
description: Aligns and reconciles data schemas across multiple systems with conflict resolution and gap analysis.
tips: Establish source authority hierarchy upfront (e.g., "Workday is the system of record for HR data, SAP for finance"). Provide sample records from each source. For large-scale integration, start with a pilot of 50-100 records.
